examples: tenpo ala la mi **weka** tan olin mi. | I would never **abandon** my partner.
mi **kama pali ala** e ilo mi. wile pali mi li weka. | I **abandoned** my coding project because I lost motivation.

eng: having the capability, skill, strength, or power to do something
examples: mi wile **ken** toki Nijon. | I want to be **able** to speak Japanese.

lipu ni li **toki e** kala. | This is a book **about** fish.
mi **toki lon** kili **lon** nasin moku ona. | I'm presenting **about** fruits and vegetables and their consumption methods.
mi pilin ike tawa kama ni. | I'm mad **about** this occurrence.

mi kute e ona tan sike nanpa 2006 **anu poka**. | I've been listening to it since **about** 2006.
lete pi tenpo lon en lete pi tenpo sike pini li **sama** lili. | It’s **about** as cold as it was last winter.

eng: to be located overhead of something else
examples: pan li lon supa **sewi** tawa supa namako. | The cereal is on the shelf **above** the spice shelf.

eng: to be intently uninvolved in something due to social status or similar
examples: mi ante tawa ona li pali sama ala. | We are not like them. We are **above** their behavior.

eng: based on what is said by
examples: **toki** pi jan sona **la** mi ale li moli lon suno kama. | **According to** scientists, we'll all die tomorrow.

eng: through to the other side of
examples: nasin telo ni li suli a la ni li wile e wawa mute: jan li **tawa poka ante** ona. | Because this river is so wide, it's very difficult for people to go **across**.
mi awen sona e ni lon ale: mama mi li pana e olin tawa mi. | I will always remember the **acts** of love from my parents.

eng: a performer of music; a musician or band
examples: **jan** mute li pana e kalama ona lon tenpo ni! | Many **acts** will perform at this event!
ijo suli li kama lon **insa** pi tenpo musi. | There's a pivotal scene in the second **act**.

eng: to [pretend], to conceal one's true intent
examples: jan lawa li kama! ale o **len e wile** ike ona! | The police are coming! Everyone, **act** non-suspiciously!
tenpo poka la ona li kama utala e jan ale. | They've been **acting** hostile around everybody lately.

tenpo mute la mi nasin ni: musi la mi **kama jan pi lon ala**, li pana e nasin ona e pilin ona e ale ona tawa lukin. mi open ni lon tenpo weka. | I do the following fairly often: For fun, I **become a fictional person**, and I demonstrate their ways, their feelings, everything about them to onlookers. I've been doing it for a long time.
mi wile tawa ma weka. wile mi ni li open lon tenpo weka a. tenpo poka la mi kama ken **lon** e ona. | I've wanted to go to a faraway place for so long. Recently, I finally got to **act** it **out**.
sina o **pali**! | You must take **action**!

eng: being in a situation, especially military combat

examples: toki ona li sama ala pali ona **lon**. | What he said is different from what he **actually** did.
moku ni li pona mute **a**! | This food is **actually** so good!

eng: to put a thing with another, increasing the amount, or improving the whole
examples: o **pana e** telo mama tawa pan. | **Add** milk to the cereal.

**toki** ona la mi o kute ala e jan ike. | Her **advice** was to ignore the haters.
mi alasa pali e lipu suwi li pakala e pali lon sike. o pana e **nasin** tawa mi. | I'm trying to make origami, but I keep failing. Could you give me **advice**?

eng: to alter or make a difference to something
examples: ni li **ante** e kulupu kala. | This will **affect** the trout population.

tok: ike e pilin, ante e pilin
examples: moli pi mama ona li **ike** suli **e pilin** pi jan Maki. | The death of his parents greatly **affected** Makil.

tok: ni li pini la, pini ni la
eng: occurring at a point of time following a reference
examples:
mi moku e kili. **ni li pini, la** sijelo li pilin pona. | I ate some fruit. **After,** I was full.
mi tawa kulupu sona nanpa. **pini ni la** mi ken weka tan ma sona ni. | I'm going to math class. **After** that I'll be able to leave this school.
**ale la**, mi jan taso. | I'm only human **after all**.

eng: part of the day after morning but before evening
examples: mi la **insa pi tenpo suno** li pona tawa telo suwi. open suno en tenpo pi kama pimeja la, tenpo pi pona ala. | In my opinion, the **afternoon** is a good time to take sweet drinks. Mornings and evenings, not so much.

mi weka lili li kama **sin**. | I went away for a little bit and came back **again**.
**sin** la mi wile toki e ni: mi utala ala. mi wile sona taso. | Once **again**, I'd like to make it clear I'm not picking a fight, I just want to understand.

ona li **utala** e nasin ni: jan li jo e jan ante li lawa ale e ona. | He fought **against** slavery.
kulupu lawa li weka e mani mi **lon ala wile** mi. | The government is taking my money **against** my will.

eng: amount of time someone has been alive
examples:
jan li **lili** sama sina la ona o lon ala tomo ni a! | People your **age** shouldn't be in this building!
ona li kama wan lon olin la ona li **suli** seme? | What **age** were they when they married?
toki kulupu la jan li kama **majuna** la ona li kama sona. | They say that wisdom comes with **age**.
**tenpo** lon la jan ale li ken lukin e sona ale lon ilo. | We're in the Information **Age**.

eng: previously (marking a point in the past)
examples: suno **pini** nanpa tu wan la mi tawa tenpo musi. | Three days **ago** I went to a concert.
mi ale li **pilin sama**. pini li pini. | I think we **agree**: the past is over.

tenpo mute la mi pilin ala e ni lon insa: **kon** en telo li jaki ala lon ma mi. | We often take for granted the clean **air** and water in our city.
kulupu waso li open e tawa **kon** ona. | The flock of birds took to the **air**.
**pilin** la ona li wawa tan sijelo suli ona. | His broad shoulders gave him an **air** of strength and reliability.

ilo **ale** li wile e wawa. | **All** implements require some kind of energy.
mi **ale** li pilin pona tan sitelen musi. | We **all** enjoyed the movie.

eng: immune sensitivity to a given stimulus, often foods or pollen, potentially dangerous
examples: mi ken ala moku e kili kiwen. ona li pini e uta mi la mi ken ala esun e kon. | I have an **allergy** to peanuts. They close up my throat, so I wouldn't be able to breathe.

eng: to give permission or make possible
examples: mama li **ken e** len noka pi jaki ala taso lon insa tomo. | The parents only **allow** clean shoes inside the house.

**tenpo poka kama la**, ona tu li awen olin lon sike tu wan. | They've been together for **almost** three years.
jan mute la tomo li **poka** tawa ni: jan sin ala li ken kama. | The stadium was **almost** full.

mi **taso** lon ma lete. | I'm **alone** in the cold.
pini pi tenpo musi la jan Isote li pilin pi **wan taso.** | At the end of the party, Isolde felt all **alone.**

eng: on the edge of, lengthwise next to
examples: mi tawa **lon** ma jelo pi poka telo. | We're talking a walk **along** the beach.
mi kute e kalama musi la mi kalama **kin** e toki. | When I'm listening to a fun song, I sing **along** to the lyrics.

eng: having happened in the past, perhaps unexpectedly
examples: nasa la mi pilin e ni: ni li kama **lon tenpo pini**. | It's strange but I feel like this has **already** happened.
sina awen pali e tomo sina. lon la ona li **kama** pona a tawa lukin. | You're still renovating your house, but it's **already** looking great.
mi **kin** li tawa. | I'm **also** leaving.

tok: kin la, namako la, sama la
examples: ona li pona tawa lukin mi. **kin** la soweli ona li suwi. | I find him handsome, **and also** his cat is adorable.
sewi li pimeja ike. **taso** mi awen musi. | **Although** the sky is a dismal gray, I'm still having fun.
**ale la** mi olin e sina. | I will **always** love you.
**tenpo** mute a la sina lukin e ilo ike sina! | You're **always** on that damn phone!

eng: impress someone, generally by a difficult act
examples: mi tawa sike e sijelo mi la jan lukin li **pilin wawa**! | When I do cartwheels, everyone is **amazed**!
kalama li seme tawa sina? — a, mi **pilin suli ala** tawa ona. | How'd you like the album? — Eh, I'm kinda **ambivalent** about it.
sike li **lon** kulupu pi lipu kasi. | The ball was **among** the leaves.
**suli** pi jaki ma li pakala e sijelo jan. | The **amount** of pollution in the world threatens people's health.
mi sona ala e **mute** soweli. | I'm not sure about the **amount** of cows.

mi **en** sina o weka tan ma ni. | You **and** I should run away.
telo suli la telo **li** kama **li** weka. | Ocean tides come **and** go.
**o** pali e lipu sina **o** lape. | Do your homework **and** go to bed.
mi wan **e** pan **e** ko **e** kasi. | I make a sandwich out of bread, cheese, **and** salad.
len ni li pona **lon** seli **lon** lete kin. | This outfit is suitable both for hot **and** cold weather.
mi kama la mi wile moku e ko li wile anpa e sina ale. ko mi li pini. | I have come here to chew bubblegum and kick ass, **and** I'm all out of bubblegum.

eng: divine figure sent by a deity
examples: **jan** Kapike li toki e wile sewi tawa kulupu jan. | The **angel** Gabriel announces God's will to humanity.

eng: someone who is cute, innocent or kind
examples: lape mi li pini la sina pana e moku tawa mi la sina **suwi mute** a. | Thanks for making me breakfast in bed, you little **angel**.
mi weka e moku soweli la ona li wile **utala** e mi... | I **angered** the dog by taking their food...
poki telo olin pi jan poka mi li weka la ona li **pilin utala** lili. | My friend's favorite mug is gone, so they're a bit **angry**.

eng: a member of the Animalia kingdom
examples: kijetesantakalu li **soweli**. | Raccoons are **animals**.
o pana e poki telo **sin** tawa mi. | Please grab me **another** cup of tea.
musi ni li ike a. mi o lukin e **ante**. | This movie was very boring. We should've watched **another**.
mi wile e sona tan mama: jan sin li kama tan seme? **sona kama** ona li nasa tawa mi. | I asked my parents: Where to babies come from? Their **answer** surprised me.
ona li **toki** e nanpa wawa tawa alasa sona mi. | He **answered** my question with amazing statistics.

tok: ken e toki, kute e wile toki
examples: ala li **ken e toki**. ken la ona li lape. | No one is **answering**. They might be asleep.

eng: of a set, all possibilities are acceptable
examples:
jan **ale** li ken ni. | **Any** person can do that.
nasin **ale** li ken. | **Any** approach will do.

eng: (indefinite article used in questions or negative statements)
examples:
sina wile ala wile e sona namako? | Do you have **any** further questions?
mi sona ala a e tan. | I don't have **any** clue why.

mi **kama** olin **ala** e sina. mi weka. | I don't love you **anymore**. Goodbye.
ijo li ante lon insa mi. pilin monsuta mi **li weka**. | Something must have changed in me. I don't fear it **anymore**.

eng: of a set of individuals, all possibilities are acceptable
examples: jan **ale** li ken ni. | **Anyone** can do that.

eng: of a set of objects, all possibilities are acceptable
examples: **ijo ale** li moku pona tawa mi. | I'll eat **anything**.

sina ken awen lon weka. **taso** sina kama a. | You didn't have to come, but you did **anyway**.
jan mute la moku ni li ike. taso mi **awen** wile moku e ona. | I've heard this food tastes bad, but I want to try it **anyway**.

eng: (discourse marker introducing a new conversation topic)
examples: mi pali pona e moku. **ante la**, nasin sin sina kili li seme a. | I think our food turned out great. **Anyway**, what was that about your apple experiments?

jan utala o lon **ala** supa **poka**. | **Set** the two squabblers **apart**.
mi en sina li **weka** mute. jan li ken ala sona e **weka** pi suli ni. taso a, mi pilin e ni: awen la mi ken kama pona. | We might be thousands, if not millions of miles **apart**. But, I think we're going to be okay.

mi **open** e ilo li pona e insa li wan sin e ona. | We **took** the computer **apart**, fixed it, and put it back together.
len mi li kama lon palisa la ona li **tu pakala** e ona. | My shirt got caught on a pole, which **tore** it **apart**.
sitelen li toki ike **ala** e meli **la** ona li musi mute a. | **Apart from** the misogynistic jokes, the movie was pretty funny.

eng: to show up, to become present or visible
examples: ni la jan moli li **kama** a!! | And then a skeleton **appeared**!!
**lukin la** mi ken ala pini e musi ni. | This puzzle **appears** impossible.

eng: a very sweet, crisp, and crunchy fruit with a berry-like aroma
examples: jan Saja li jo e **kili** tu wan. jan Awan li jo e **kili** luka tu. ona li wan e **kili** ale, la **kili** pi mute seme li lon? | Sarai has three **apples.** Abram has seven **apples.** If they combine all their **apples, how many **apples** are there?
nimi 'suwi' li toki **pona** e ona. | "Kind-hearted" is an **appropriate** description of him.
ma Awawi li **kulupu pi nena mute** li tan seli pi anpa ma. | Hawaii is an **archipelago** formed by volcanic activity.

tok: ike a, seme a, pakala a
examples: **seme a**? kiwen lili li weka ike tawa insa pi ilo suli a! | **Are you kidding me**? Now I've dropped the screw into the machine!

tok: seme a, sina toki e seme
eng: (expressing confusion for what was just said)
examples: **sina toki e seme?** suwi mi li ken ala ni. | **Are you kidding me?** My baby would never do that.
mi kama lon **ma** pi kasi nasa mute. | We found ourselves in an **area** where strange vegetation was abound.
sitelen li pimeja ike lon ni. | The picture is too dark in that **area**.
supa lape sina li **suli** seme? o nanpa e ona. | Measure the **area** of your bed.
ike la mama mi li **toki utala** lon tenpo mute. | I hate how my parents are always **arguing**.

eng: an appendage attached to the shoulders
examples: mi pana **luka** e telo tawa uta sina. | I shall pour water into your mouth by means of my **arms.**
mi pana e kili suwi **lon** ale pi **selo** pan. | I placed cute strawberries all **around** the cake's perimeter.

tok: lon selo, lon weka, lon ala
eng: on the edge of or away from
examples:
mi tawa **lon selo** pi supa awen. | We walked **around** the parking lot.
nasin suli li pini la mi tawa **lon** ona **ala**. | The highway was closed, so I made sure to drive **around** it.
sona li **lon ale** pi tomo ni. sina wan e ona ale la sina ken weka. | There are clues scattered **around** the room. If you find them all, you'll be able to leave.
sina **lon** ma ni lon tenpo mute anu seme? | Are you **around** this place often?
kulupu pona li **lon** la tenpo moku li kama pona. | Dinnertime is better when friends are **around**.
tenpo mute la mi toki ala e pali mi. kulupu o pilin ala e ni: **mi alasa toki suli e mi lon ale**. | I don't talk about my achievements much because I don't want to come off as **arrogant**.

eng: visual art; the practice of drawing, painting, sculpting, etc.
an instance of visual art; a drawing, painting, sculpture, etc.
examples: mi pilin pona wawa tan **sitelen** sina! | I love your **art**!

eng: something subjective or inexact (as opposed to [science])
examples: mama mi la, nasin pi seli moku li **tan pilin**. | For my nonna, cooking is an **art**.
tomo mi **la** ona li suli ala. | It's not **as** large **as** my house.
mi sona mama e toki Inli li pilin e ni: nasin toki ni li nasa. | **As** a native English speaker, I find that an odd way to say it.

eng: in the manner of, in the way of
examples: mi ken e weka pi jan awen, **sama** wile sina. | I will release the prisoners, **as** you wish.

eng: dust formed when something burns, often white or gray
examples: seli li pini. **ko** taso li awen lon poki. | The fire burned out. Only **ash** remains in the brazier.

jan lawa li **wile sona** e ni: jan seme li pilin ike li pali ala? | The manager **asked** who was out sick.
jan pakala li **wile** e telo. | The injured person **asked** for a glass of water.
sina **toki e wile** la mi pana. | **Ask** and you shall receive.
**o** weka. | I kindly **ask** you to leave.
mi tawa anpa li pakala e **monsi** mi! | I fell down and hurt my **ass**!
mi pali e ko tan telo **soweli**. | I made cheese from **goat** milk.

eng: in or near a place or time
examples: mi **lon** tomo awen pi ilo tawa. | I'm **at** the bus stop.
o pana ala wawa e kiwen **tawa** jan. | Don't throw rocks **at** people.

eng: the gas above the ground surrounding a planet
examples: **kon sewi** li sama len mute poka. | The **atmosphere** is like many layers of cloth.
mi kama lon kulupu musi, li **pilin** e nasa lon ona, li weka. | I felt a strange **atmosphere** when I arrived at the party, so I decided to skedaddle.

eng: to take a violent or aggressive action against another being or entity
examples:
suno ni la ma Juwese li **kama utala** e ma Kanata. | The United States **attacked** Canada today.
jan li toki ike e tonsi la mi **utala** e ona. | If someone says something transphobic, I'll **attack** them.

mi **kute** ala e toki pi jan sona. | I wasn't **paying attention** to the lecturer.
mi **ken** ala awen **kute** e ijo lon tenpo suli. | I have a short **attention** span.
**o kute a**! | sona ni li suli. | **Attention** please! This is really important.

eng: ability or permission to be in command
examples:
sitelen li suno la wawa li tawa lon insa sina. sina **ken**. | As the symbol glows, power courses through you. **Authority**.
open la ona li toki utala e ni: ona a li **lawa** e kulupu. | He asserted his **authority** over the team right from the start.

tok: ante lawa, lawa (li) ante, nasa lawa, lawa (li) nasa; nasin lawa (ante) la ...
eng: a type of neurodivergency characterized by divergence in social situations, among many other symptoms
examples:
**lawa** mi li **ante**. | I am **autistic**.
**nasin lawa** mi la moku mute li pana e pilin jaki tawa uta mi. | I'm **autistic**, so many foods will have an unpleasant texture to me.

eng: not sleeping or in a coma, conscious
examples: sewi li pimeja. taso ona **li lape ala**. | It is nighttime, but she is **awake**.
o **weka**! weka ala la mi toki utala sin e sina. | Now go **away**, or I shall taunt you a second time.
ilo tawa li wile e telo wawa la tomo telo li lon **weka** ike. | If we need to refuel, the nearest gas station is miles **away**.

eng: a very young animal, before being a child
examples: **jan lili** li musi kepeken leko. | The **baby** is playing with blocks.

eng: the side opposite to the front
examples: sitelen musi li lon **monsi** pi tomo ni. | There's a mural on the **back** of that building.

eng: the back part of the body
examples: a, **monsi** mi li pilin pakala. | Ugh, my **back** hurts.
tenpo ale la jan **ike** li kama lawa e ma. | It is always **bad** people who get to rule.
tenpo li weka la moku li kama **jaki**. | Given enough time food goes **bad**.
ona li wile **a** utala lon poka. | He **badly** wanted to join the fight.

tok: selo palisa kon, ilo kalama kon, etc.
eng: a large instrument composed of a skin that is filled with air and squeezed into usually four pipes: three drones and one chanter
examples:
"a, lipamanka li sona e nasin pi **selo palisa kon"** | "Oh lipamanka knows how to play **bagpipes."**
**selo pi palisa luka** li kalama ike tawa mi. | I hate the way the **bagpipes** sound. 
**ilo kalama kon selo** li kama pakala, la mi mu musi pi pilin pona. | When **bagpipes** break, I laugh of joy.

eng: a spherical object small enough to be held in hand
examples: musi la jan li noka e **sike** tawa poki ni sinpin. | In the game, you kick the **ball** to that goal in front of us.

eng: a long segmented plant indigenous to Asia
examples: sina sona ala sona e ni: **kasi palisa** li lili, la jan li ken moku e ona a! | Did you know that very young **bamboo** is edible?

tomo soweli pi pona sijelo li pana e **kili palisa jelo** pi mani ala la jan Sojana li kama jo e kili ni luka tu. | The vet was giving out free **bananas,** so Johanna took seven. 
mi esun e **kili palisa** sin laso li weka e ona. |  I bought green **bananas**, then forgot about them. 
sona sina la, moku pi **kili palisa** pimeja li ken ala ken pakala e jan? | Do you know whether black **bananas** are safe to eat?

eng: the bottom, supporting edge of something
examples: **anpa** pi sitelen jan kiwen li pakala la ona li ken ala awen sinpin li tawa anpa taso. | The mannequin's **base** is broken so it just falls over.

eng: a strategically-located structure that holds resources
examples:
lon anpa pi nena Somolama la jan Masa li lape lon **tomo.** | Marshall slept at the **base** located at the bottom of Mount Everest.
ma Mewika li wile len e sona ni: **tomo** ona li lon ma Akensina. | The United States wants to hide that they have a **base** operating in Argentina.

eng: to design with something as a prototype
examples: mi **pali** e ilo ni **tan** sitelen pi jan Pensiman. | I **based** this tool **on** Benjamin's designs.

eng: politically aligned with the speaker (originally from 4chan)
examples: nasin ona li **pona** a. | She has **based** opinions.
**waso** li mu, la ona li ken lukin kute. | When **bats** make noises, they can see with their ears.

eng: a large stick used for baseball and as a weapon
examples: mi utala **palisa** e sina lon tenpo ni: sina toki jaki e mije mi. | I'll hit you with my **bat** the moment you talk shit about my husband.
meli lili li **pini sike e lukin** ona la o sona e ni: ona li ken ala pali ike. | When a young girl **bats** her eyes at you, you know that she can do no wrong.

eng: to push something away with the hand in quick motions
examples: mi **luka** e sina tan ni: mi wile weka e sina. | I'm **batting** you away because I want you to go away.

eng: a receptacle for cleaning one's body, typically while reclined
examples: mi lon **poki telo** la mi ken ala toki uta tan nasa. | I'm in the **bath,** so I won't join voice chat because that would be weird.
mi **telo e mi** la kon mi li pona tawa nena. | When I **bathe**, I smell good.

eng: a room deticated to removal of waste from inside one's body and grime from the flesh
examples: suno ni la mi tawa **tomo jaki** lon tenpo mute ale tu tu, taso sina ken ala sona e tan. | Today I went to the **bathroom** two thousand and four times, but you're not allowed to know why.

mi wile moku. | I **am** hungry.
sina o sina. | Just **be** yourself.

mi pilin la mi **lon**. | I think, therefore I **am**.
mi wile **lon** supa pi jan majuna. | I want **to be** at the grown up's table.

eng: indicating material or means of manufacture
examples:
sinpin ni li kiwen wawa la mi ken ala pakala e ona. | The door **is made of** solid steel, so we won't be able to break it down.
mi **pali e** poki ni **kepeken** linja kasi a. | This basket **is made of** straw.

eng: a large mammal with shaggy fur, a small tail, and flat feet
examples:
mi kama alasa e soweli. | We're going on a bear hunt.
o luka ala e soweli suli. | Don't touch the bear.
lon ma Amelika lete la soweli pimeja li moku e kasi e soweli kin. | In North America, black bears are omnivores.
soweli pimeja walo ni li moku ala e kasi palisa ona tan seme? | Why is the panda not eating its bamboo?
mi pilin monsuta mute tan soweli walo pi ma lete. | I have a fear of polar bears.

eng: koala bear, a tree-dwelling mammal native to Australia
examples: jan Minsen li jo e luka mi sama soweli lape pi ma Oselija. | Minsin clasped my arm like a koala.

tok: mije suli pi linja sijelo mute
examples: mi mije suli pi linja sijelo mute li wile unpa pi tenpo lili e mije ante mute. | I'm a bear seeking hook-ups.

jan Santa li jo e ilo musi mute. | Santa bears many presents.
jan lili tu wan li lon insa pi jan mama. | The expecting parent is bearing triplets.
noka ona li awen e linja kipisi pi tenpo ike pakala ona. | Her legs bear the scars of her trauma.
nasin linja la mi kama lon ma kasi pi **pona lukin**. | The bending road led us through a **beautiful** valley of flowers.
mi kute e kalama pi jan Petopen. ona li **pona** a **tawa kute** mi. | I listened to Beethoven's most **beautiful** sonata.
a, ilo sina li pali kepeken tenpo lili. pali **wawa** a!  | Wow, your code is so efficient. **Beautiful** work!
mi o esun e ilo telo sin **tan ni**: ike la ilo mi li pana lili e telo lon tenpo ale. | I need to purchase a new kitchen tap, **because** mine leaks constantly.

eng: on account of, for the purpose of
examples: mi tawa tomo mama mi **tan** moku. | I'm visiting my parents **because of** food.
mi wile ala **kama** sama mama mi. | I don't want to **become** like my father.

examples: jan pi ma Isijopija ala li sona ala pali e ko loje. ona li pilin e ni: ko loje ni li sama ko loje pi ma ante. ni li lon ala. | Non-Ethiopians don't know how to make **berbere.** They think that this red spice blend is like any other country's red spice blend. This is not true.

ni li nasin **nanpa wan** pi pali tomo. | This is the **best** way to build a house.

ni li **suli** ala tawa mi. | No **big deal**.

ilo nanpa li sona e nanpa kepeken kulupu pi **wan** luka tu wan. | Computers store data in groups of 8 **bits**.

mi pali e **nasin** ni: tenpo pimeja li kama la sinpin ilo li kama jelo. | I'm writing the **code** for a blue-light filter.
kulupu ISO li pana e **nanpa** 'tok' tawa toki pona. | The ISO assigned the **code** 'tok' to Toki Pona.

eng: number or password used for access control
examples: jan li pana e **nanpa** pona la poki li open. | When someone enters the correct **code**, the box opens.
**toki len** li ken lon nanpa ni. | There might be a **code** among these numbers.
**nasin** pi kulupu utala la mi ike ala e jan. | Not hurting anyone is part of the gang's **code**.
mi **pali e ilo** ni: ona li ken wan e nanpa. | I'm **coding** a calculator.

eng: to encode, conceal with a cipher
examples: ona li toki **len**. mi ken ala sona e ona. | They're sending **coded** messages that we can't decipher.

tok: telo, telo pi weka lape, telo pimeja, telo seli, telo wawa
eng: hot black beverage that prevents tiredness
examples: o toki ala tawa mi lon tenpo ni: mi moku ala e **telo wawa** mi a! | Don't talk to me until I've had my **coffee!**

kon li **lete** a lon weka tomo! o len seli e sina. | It's very **cold** outside! Make sure to wear warm clothes.
**lete** li ken pakala e ilo. | The **cold** can cause machines to malfunction.
jan lukin li sona pona ala e ni: **kule** seme li lon selo pi ilo tawa. | The witness couldn't say for a fact what **color** the car was.
tenpo seme la sina **kama**? | When did you **come**?
ona li **toki e** tonsi ona tawa mi. | They **came out** to me as trans.

eng: a group appointed to make a decision in tandem
examples: **kulupu** li toki e ni: pali sina li pini la sina o tawa lupa seli. | The **committee** has spoken: when you finish working, you shall go to the fire pit.
**tenpo mute la** mi lukin e kala alasa lon selo telo. | It's **common** to see sharks at the coastline.
**kulupu** mute pi toki pona li lon. | There are many Toki Pona **communities**.

eng: a group of people working together under a legal entity
examples: mama mi li open e **kulupu** lili pi meli taso. | My mother started a small **company** of only women.
mi toki tawa **lawa** la ona li wile weka e mi tan ni: nanpa mi li lili ike. | When I talked to the **company,** they told me that they're firing me because my number is too low.
mi **alasa e ante** lon lipu ona lon lipu Wikipesija. ken la pali ona li tan lipu ni. | I'm **comparing** their essay to a Wikipedia article. Maybe their work is based off it.

tok: alasa e pona, e ijo pi pona nanpa wan
examples: mi tawa esun tu wan li **alasa** e sona ni: moku pi wile mi la ona seme li wile e mani pi **lili nanpa wan**? | I'm **comparing** the prices in three different grocery stores.
mi en jan poka mi li lon **utala** ni: jan seme li ken mu pi wawa nanpa wan? | My friend and I are in a **competition** to see who can yell the loudest.

eng: to assemble works or information into a single document
examples: jan seme li **wan** e lipu ale ni? | Who **compiled** this anthology?

eng: (of a computer) to read input files of code, validate them, and convert them into simpler output files
examples: ilo mi li **nasin** e toki ilo. | My computer **compiles** code.

eng: taking extra mental capacity to understand
examples: toki pi jan ni li **suli nasa** la mi ken ala sona. | This person's speech was so **complex** that I couldn't understand it.

eng: a large building, e.g. an apartment complex
examples:
mi lon **tomo suli** pi jan mute. | I'm in the apartment **complex**.
**tomo suli** la kulupu olin pi jan utala en jan utala pi olin ala li ken lon. | The **complex** houses married and single soldiers.

mi alasa e sona lon **ilo**. | I'm doing **computer** research.
sina lukin e sitelen suwi lon **ilo**. | You're looking at cute pictures on the **computer**.
mi en jan olin mi li lon weka. **ilo** la mi awen ken toki. | Me and my friends are far from each other. Thanks to **computers**, we can still talk.

eng: a desktop or laptop computer, in contrast to e.g. a smartphone
examples: mi wile sitelen mute la ilo lili li pona ala la mi o kepeken **ilo suli**. | Smaller devices aren't great for long-form writing, so I should use my **computer**.
mi kepeken ilo kule wawa mute lon musi Manka la, **ilo nanpa** mi li wile moli pakala. | When I use a lot of shader mods in Minecraft, my **computer** wants to explode.
ilo sin li wile ala e mani mute tan ni: tenpo esun la ona li pakala mute. | My computer wasn't very expensive because it was in very poor **condition** when I bought it.

mi tawa mute ala e **sijelo** mi la ona li kama **ike**. | My physical **condition** went downhill when I stopped exercising.
tan **nasin** pi **pilin** mi la mi ken ala tawa e ijo suli. | I'm not able to carry heavy things because of my **heart condition**.

meli lawa lili li weka e jan tan poki **lon ni taso:** ona o pana e suwi tawa meli lon suno ale. | The princess released her victims, but **on the condition that** they'd have to bake her a cupcake every day.
kon seli en kon lete li kama wan. ni la utala li ken kama lon sewi. | Hot and cold air are intermixing. Under those **conditions**, a thunderstorm can form.

eng: a language deliberately created by a person
examples: jan li wile kama sona e **toki pali** tan seme? | What makes people want to learn **conlangs**?

eng: think about, especially to come to a decision
examples: sina wile ala wile esun? o **pilin** lon ni, o kama sin lon tenpo suno kama. | Do you want to buy? **Consider** it, and come back tomorrow.

tok: poka; {ijo, ale, tenpo, toki, sona, ...} poka
eng: prior knowledge of a conversation's participants, or current knowledge about their surroundings
examples:
**toki ni** la 'ilo' li toki e ilo ni: ona li lon luka mi. | In **this context**, 'ilo' meant the tool I was holding.
toki mi la ona o lukin e ilo pali. tan **tenpo poka** ona li sona e ni: ilo li kama pali e kiwen a lon tenpo poka. ona o lukin e ona. | I told it to check on the printer. In context, it knew that I meant the 3D printer where I'd just started a new print.

eng: to influence, to hold authority over
examples:
jan wan taso li ken ala **lawa** e toki pona. | A single person cannot **control** Toki Pona.
mi **lawa** ala e ni: kala li kama moli lon tenpo seme. | I do not **control** the speed at which lobsters die.
mi en sina la seme li **lawa**? | Which of us is in **control**?
jan Lanu li **seli** e moku pona. | Ranu **cooks** good food.
**pali** mi li **wawa ala wawa**? | Am I **cooking**?

eng: someone who prepares food, usually as a job
examples: **pali** mi la mi **seli e moku**. | I'm a **cook** by profession.

jan sitelen ni li lon: ona li lukin e sitelen wawa li **pali e sitelen sama**. | Some artists **copy** famous paintings.
o **mute** e lipu pi wile sona. | Can you **copy** the survey?

eng: (on a computer) to save to the clipboard without deleting the original text

examples: sina en jan poka sina li **sama** a! | You and your sibling are exact **copies**!

tok: kili pan; sike kili jelo lili mute
eng: a starchy grain crop, often yellow, or its seeds
examples: o pana e **kili lili jelo** tawa pan mi. | Please put some **corn** on my pizza.

eng: eliciting a weak negative emotional reaction due to a cliche nature
examples: toki ale pi mama mi li **nasa ike.** ona li toki e olin taso! | Everything my grandma says is so **corny**. She only talks about love!
mi lon **ma** Tosi. | I'm in (the **country** of) Germany.
kulupu Katala li wile kama **lawa** e **ma** ona. | Catalans want to form their own **country**.

ona li pana e kiwen suwi **mute** tawa mi. | He handed me a **couple** pieces of candy.
ona li ante **lili** e lipu open mi. | He made a **couple** of changes to my first draft.
supa **tu** li lon poka. | A **couple** of tables stood next to each other.

eng: a group of people in love
examples:
sina en jan Se I li **olin** anu seme? | Are you and Jae Yee a **couple**?
tenpo musi pi pimeja lon li tawa **jan olin** taso. | Tonight's dance party is for **couples** only.
ilo wawa li **wan** e tomo tu. | The trains are **coupled** by a strong connector.
kulupu li tawa lon **ma** nena. | They were hiking along a bumpy **course**.

eng: one of several servings of food eaten in succession
examples: moku open li ko. **moku** suli li waso. | The appetizer was some yogurt, and the main **course** will be a turkey.
o kama **sona** mama e kasi lili kepeken **sitelen** mi! | Learn how to care for a bonsai with my video **course**!
ona li **len** e jan lili. | He **covered** the baby with a blanket.

eng: hidden, invisible, clandestine, with an objective not to be discovered
examples: **len** la ona li pana e moku meli tawa meli tonsi pi kulupu ona. | She runs a **covert** operation supplying transgender women in her community with estrogen.

tok: soweli, soweli pimeja walo, soweli pi telo walo moku
eng: a black and white mammal, often used by humans for its milk
examples:
**soweli** tu li mu e mu mu. | **Two** cows moo: moo, moo.
suno ale la **soweli** li pana sin e **telo** pona tawa kulupu mi. | A milk **cow** provides fresh, creamy milk for our family every day.

eng: to collide (of a physical object) with something
examples:
ilo tawa sina li **pakala** e mi, la ona li ilo tawa ala li kiwen pakala. | When your car hit me, it wasn't a vehicle, it was a bludgeoning projectile. 
jan lili mute li lukin ala e poka ona la ona li **sijelo e** jan ante mute. | Some children don't pay attention to their surroundings, so they **crash into** a lot of other people.

eng: to create a loud noise due to physical impact
examples: sike jelo tu li **kalama wawa** lon kulupu pi ilo kalama. | The cymbals **crashed** in the orchestra.

eng: to cease working in unplanned fashion (of a computer)
examples: a, musi Manka li **kama pakala** lon tenpo ni: mi kama e ijo tawa mute. | Ugh, Minecraft **crashed** when I spawned in too many entities.

eng: to show up uninvited to a party or event (often to distrupt it in some way)
examples: mi wile ala e ni: jan Makame li sona e kulupu musi. ona li sona, la ona li **kama lon** li **utala.** | I don't want Muhamad to know about the party because if he does know about it, he'll **crash** it.

eng: to immediately fall asleep or otherwise enter a relative state of rest after either a lot of activity or recreational drug use (of a person)
examples: olin o, mi olin mute sewi wawa pi pona epiku nanpa wan e sina, taso mi pali lon tenpo suli li wile **kama lape.** | My love. I love you so much above all with passion epicly the best. But I worked so much today and I really just need to **crash.**

eng: to trend low in number (of a stock or the stock market)
examples: jan ike li kama nasin e esun la mani ale li **kama lili nanpa.** | If a bad person is put in charge of the market, all the stocks will **crash.**

tok: nasin pi kama sona, nasin pi tenpo lili
eng: a quick method of learning about a topic, usually surface level
examples: mi o pana e sona toki tawa sina kepeken **nasin pi tenpo lili.** | I should teach you linguistics using a **crash course.**
jan sona li **pali** e len sin. | Skilled workers **create** new kinds of garments.

sewi li **lon** e jan. | The divine **created** a person.
ma tomo mi la jan lawa li **lon** e ma luka wan. | The mayor of my town **created** six wards.
moli pan li **kama** e moli jan. | Crop failures **created** famine.

eng: to engage in something creative, imaginative
examples: **musi** li pona tawa jan lili. jan suli li ken ala kepeken ijo musi ona la ni li suli ala a. | The children enjoy **creating**, never mind if it's of any use to grownups!

eng: able or willing to create new things
examples: lawa mi li **wile pali e ijo sin** lon tenpo mute. | I have a very **creative** mind.
mi lukin ala e nasin **musi** ni pi nimi 'kala' lon ale pi tenpo pini. | I've never seen 'kala' used in such a **creative** way before.

tok: seli (e) la ona li kama ko, ko e
eng: (of a body) to burn to ash
examples:
sina moli, la mi **seli** e sijelo sina, **la ona li kama ko,** la mi pana e ko lon telo. | When you die, I'll **cremate** you, and scatter your ashes into the ocean.
mama mi li moli la mi **ko** e sijelo ona li pana e ko lon poki. | When my mother passed away, I **cremated** her and put the ashes in a jar.

tok: kute ala e lawa, ike tawa lawa, pakala lawa
examples: lawa li ike, la jan pona o **pakala lawa.** | When the law is bad, good people do **crime.**

eng: disgust and aversion to a stimulus
examples: mi kama lukin e sijelo pini mi pi ilo Jutu la mi **pilin nasa ike** li **wile weka** tan lukin. | When I come upon my old Youtube account, I **cringe**.
mi lukin e **soweli** suwi lon nasin! o lukin e sitelen a. | Look, I saw this cute **critter** on the street!
jan lawa **ike** li anpa e jan pali li weka e mani ona. | The **cruel** capitalists oppress the proletariat and take their money.

tok: pana e telo lukin, telo li kama tan lukin
examples:
mama pi jan Sola li **pana e telo tan lukin** li mu pi pilin ike. | Sora's father **cried** and moaned in agony.
sijelo mi li mu mute a tan pilin musi suli la **telo li kama tan lukin** mi. | I'm laughing so hard that I'm **crying.**

tok: wan e sijelo, poka e sijelo, poka suwi
eng: to make prolonged physical contact out of affection
examples: sina wile ala wile **poka suwi e sijelo**? | Do you want to **cuddle**?

eng: food or customs of making food, associated with a place
examples: **nasin pi ma Anku** la **moku** li ken namako kepeken ko pipi. | Korean **cuisine** sometimes incorporates shrimp paste for depth in certain dishes.
ona li sewi e **poki** ona pi **telo** wawa li toki e pona. | She raised her **cup** of mead in a toast to friendship.
**len** ni li ike a tawa selo **nena** mi. | The **cups** are made of a particularly uncomfortable material.
kulupu mi li utala pona. **utala musi** tu la mi wawa nanpa wan. | Our team went one better this year, winning both the league and the **cup**.
mi pali e ko tan **kili mute lili** li pana e ona tawa pan mi. | I made a preserve from **currants** and spread it on my toast.
mi toki e soweli **pi tenpo ni.** ona a li moli e jan poka mi! | I'm talking about the **current** dog. HE killed my friend!
mi lon telo la **telo wawa** li weka e mi tan poka ma. | When I was swimming, a **powerful current** pulled me from shore.

eng: to split into parts with a tool
examples: mi **tu** e kili la insa ona li pana e kon li jaki e lukin. | When **cutting** onions, their inside releases fumes that irritate the eyes.

eng: an continuous injury in the skin
examples: **pakala** li awen lon luka mi tan tenpo ni: mi pakala ike ilo e ona. | There's still a **cut** on my finger from when I slipped with a knife.

eng: on a computer, to remove and take onto the clipboard
examples: o **weka** e toki open o pana e ona lon pini. | **Cut** the first paragraph and paste it at the end.

eng: to remove a segment of a video or other media in the course of editing/development
examples: musi Seta la jan pali li **weka** e ijo mute tan musi. | The developers of the Zelda games **cut** a lot of content from the games.
mi alasa lili e **ken pakala** lon nasin mi ni: ona li lon sewi pi lupa suli li linja lili taso pi wawa ala. | We've tried to reduce **danger** on our flimsy rope bridge that leads over a huge cave.

sina tawa lon taso la **sina ken moli**! o jo e ni. | **It's dangerous** to go alone! Take this.
mi pana e ko suno ni tawa insa pi selo mi la **ona li ken ala ken pakala e insa mi**? | Are glow powder cuttings **dangerous**?
ilo waso pi tenpo sin la **ken pakala** li lili. | Modern planes aren't very **dangerous**.
ale ni li **pimeja**; o open e suno. | It's **dark** here, turn on the light.
mi lukin e sitelen ni: moli en pakala olin en **ijo ike** sama li lon. | I watched a movie with **dark** themes like death and emotional severance.

**nanpa** la jan li kama ken moku e kon pi kasi nasa la jan sin mute ala li kama ni. | The **data** shows no significant increase in marijuana consumption after it was legalized.
lipu li toki e sona pi ma ale la tenpo mute la **sona** li lon ala ma pi kulupu Kalali. | Many world-map infographics have no **data** for Greenland.

tok: meli, jan, kama, lili, meli kama, etc
examples:
**kama** mi li nanpa wan lon sona nanpa. | My **daughter** is the best at math. 
mi o pana poka e **meli kama** mi e **meli kama** sina tawa ni: mi ken alasa e pona e ike. | We should put my **daughter** next to your **daughter** and compare their good and bad qualities.

eng: a unit of time made up of 24 hours
examples: **suno** tu wan la mi awen, taso ona li awen toki ala tawa mi. | I've waiting for three days, but she still hasn't spoken to me.
sina o sona e ni: sina **moli** a! | Have you heard the news? You are **dead**!

eng: of a space: currently inactive, not busy
examples: a, sin la **jan ala li toki** lon kulupu ni. | Great, chat's **dead** again.
sinpin pi ilo mi li **pakala**. mi o esun e ona sin. | My monitor is **dead**. Guess I'll have to buy a new one.
**sitelen ala** li kama lon linja. ilo li pana ala pana tawa ona? | Looks like the video cable's **dead**. Can you check it's connected properly?

tok: nimi weka, nimi pini, nimi moli
eng: former name of someone who changed their name
examples: ike la lipu mute ni li toki e **nimi pini** mi la mi wile ala lukin e ona. | Unfortunately, many of these documents contain my **deadname**, so I don't really wanna look at them.

mi sona e ken **moli** li pilin ike ala tan ona. | I'm cognizant of **death** and not afraid of it.
**moli** pi jan mani li ike ala tawa kulupu. | Nobody felt sad over the rich person's **death**.

tok: alasa, sona e pona, sona e wile
examples:
jan mute li pana e sitelen tawa utala mi. mi awen **alasa** e sitelen nanpa wan. | Many people submitted drawings to our contest, and we're still **deciding** a winner.
sinpin tomo o kule ni seme? mi **sona** ala **e wile** mi a! | Which of these colors should I paint my room in? I can't **decide**!
mi pi tenpo pini li wile tawa ma Oseja. **wile** ni li awen pona tawa mi. | Past me decided to move to Australia. I still believe it was the right **decision**.
o pana e **kulupu lipu** la mi ken musi! | Get the **deck** out so we can play!

eng: the topmost walking surface of a ship, the raised surface in front of a house
examples: telo li kama lon **supa noka** tan wawa pi telo suli. | Water came onto the **deck** because of the ocean's strength.

eng: to throw (something or someone) out of a window
examples:
mi **pana lupa** e sina. | I **defenestrate** you. 
ma Pawa la jan li **pana** e jan lawa tu **tan lupa**! | In Prague, they **defenestrated** two lords regent!
telo **mute** li lon kon pi tomo ni. | There's a high **degree** of moisture in this room.

eng: extensive knowledge acquired through study and tested by an examination
examples: **sona** ni mi li **wawa**: mi pali e ilo la mi pana seme e ken ona tawa jan kepeken la ona li ken sona pona e ken e nasin li ken kepeken pona. taso mi kepeken ala sona mi a. | I have a **degree** in UX design, but I don't use it.

tok: tenpo; (tenpo) lape; tenpo li kama suli
eng: time before something happens; event that increases the time before another event happens
examples:
nasin pi sinpin mi li pakala la tomo o **lape**. ni la **tenpo** tawa mi **li kama suli**. | The train suffered **delays** due to a fault on the railroad ahead. Accordingly, our arrival was **delayed**.
mi pana e kalama la **tenpo** lili **li weka** la ona li kama sin. | The sound echoed back after a short **delay**.
mi **weka** e toki pi jan ike. | I **deleted** the bigot's comments.

tok: pali e selo, pali e nasin (lukin)
eng: to create something, especially something visual or instructions for creating another thing
examples:
mi **pali e nasin pali** sin pi ilo sitelen. | I've **designed** a new pen.
jan ni li **pali** e supa nasa. | He **designs** avant-garde furniture.
**sitelen** sin pi kulupu sina li suwi anu seme? | Your company's new **design** looks pretty nice, eh?
kasi mi li **kama suli** pona a! | My tomato plants are **developing** nicely!

mani mi la mi **pali** e ilo. | I **develop** software for a living.
mi **pali** e nasin sin tawa kulupu. | We've **developed** a new technique for the classroom.
**lipu** Kemeka li pana e **nimi** mute e toki poka ona kin. | The Kemeka **dictionary** shows words as well as the context they are used in.
ona li **moli** tan jaki pipi. | He **died** of malaria.

eng: (of a machine) to stop functioning, chiefly due to battery having been depleted (see '[dead]')
examples: a, ilo kalama mi li **moli** sin. | Oh, my headphones **died** again.
mi **wile a** toki tawa ona. | I'm **dying** to talk to her.

eng: a cube with numbers, used in games
examples: **leko** li kama tawa. | The **die** is cast.
pipi en kala li **sama ala**. | Bugs are **different** from fish.
mi o lape lon tenpo **ante**. | I should go to bed at a **different** time.

tok: wile e {pali, wawa} mute, pali suli
examples:
mi wile sona toki Sonko la ni li **wile e pali mute**. | Chinese is a **difficult** language for me to learn.
ni li **pali suli** ala, li pali nasa. | This is not **difficult**, just confusing.
pali mi la mi o lon e wile pi jan mute ni: ona li **toki ike** tawa mi li **utala** e mi. | At work, I have to deal with **difficult** customers.

eng: the practice of mediating the relationship between two or more nations
person charged with managing the relationship between two or more nations
examples: jan pi ma Sonko en jan pi ma Mewika li toki lon pali esun pi tenpo kama. | The Chinese and American **diplomats** discussed the future of trade between their countries.
ona li **toki** e tenpo ken taso. | They were **discussing** merely hypothetical scenarios.
jan sona li ken ala sona e **ike** lon **sijelo** ona. | The doctor could not diagnose their disease.
ma Lanten en ma Paki li **weka** seme? | How large is the **distance** between London and Paris?
mi kute e kalama tan **weka**. | We heard a **distant** rumbling.

sina seme? | What are you **doing**?
kon li pona e mi. | The fresh air **did** me some good.
o pali! | **Do** your job!
open pi suno ni la mi moku ala. | I **did** not eat this morning.
ona li pona ala pona tawa sina? | **Do** you like it?
ona li weka e jaki kepeken telo. mi **ni** kin. | They washed the floor, and I **did** too.
mi sona **a** e ni. | I **do** know that.
nasin li ken a pakala sin lon tenpo poka. taso tenpo lon la **ale li pona**. | The pipe will probably be leaking again soon, but **it'll do** for now.

tok: jan, jan sijelo, jan pi pona sijelo
examples: **jan ni** li pona e insa mi ni: **ona li kama e telo tan moku**. | This is my **gastrointestinologist**.

tok: jan, jan sona, jan pi {field of expertise}
eng: an expert in a field who graduated with a PhD or equivalent
examples: **jan pi sona toki** li utala mute a. | **Doctors of linguistics** very frequently debate.

eng: domesticated furry animal commonly kept as a pet
examples:
**soweli** sina li suwi a. | Your **dog** is so cute.
o lukin e **soweli tomo** mi. | Look at my **dog**.
open pi tomo mi la mi sin e **sinpin** pi lupa kama. | We installed a new front **door**.

eng: referring metonymically to the doorway itself
examples: o pini e **lupa tomo**! | Please close the **door**(way)!

tok: pan; pan suwi sike, pan pi ko insa
eng: deep-fried round pastry, often filled with jam
examples: ma Tosi la tenpo sike li pini la jan mute li moku e **pan suwi sike** li pana e seli kule lon weka tomo. | In Germany, many people eat **doughnuts** and set off fireworks on New Year's Eve.
mi **tawa anpa** tan sewi tomo la mi pilin e lili mi. | As I **went down** from the top of the building, I felt how small I was.
mi lukin **tawa anpa** lipu. | I read down the page.
suno li wawa ala la, **pilin** li kama **anpa**. | I feel **down** because there is not enough sunlight.
jan ale li **moli**. sina kama wawa. | All enemies are **down**. Level Up!
sina sewi la ni li suli ala. sina **wile ala wile** moli? | I don't care if you're an angel. Are you **down** to die?

eng: (of a server, application, or website) not running, not working, not functioning
examples:
pakala la lipu ilo mi li **pini** lon tenpo suno tu kama. | Unfortunately, our website will be **down** for the next two days.
tenpo suno ni la mi **ken** ala **lon** ilo Siko. | Discord is **down** today.
mi sona **sitelen** e waso. | I know how to **draw** birds.
o **kama jo** e lipu tu. | **Draw** two cards.

eng: to cause an interest or desire
examples: tenpo ale la mun sewi li **musi** tawa mi. | I've always **been drawn** to the night sky.

tok: pini musi pi jan sewi ala
eng: end of a game with no winner
examples: musi li kama lon ni: **jan ala li ken anpa e jan ante.** | The game came to a **draw**.

eng: to experience imaginary events in the mind while sleeping
examples: **lape** la mi ken tawa lon kon sama waso. | I **dreamed** that I could fly like a bird.
**wile** mi la ala la jan li pakala e jan ante. | I have a **dream** that people will never hurt others.
wawa a. mi **lawa** e ilo. | Dude, I'm **driving** this car.

eng: to let an object fall, often by accident
examples: jan o! ilo sina li **weka** tan sina. | Hey you! You **dropped** your pen.
mi o **awen ala toki** e ni. | Let's **drop** the subject.
**telo** ni li ken pona e lape sina. o moku e ona **lili** tu wan lon open pi tenpo lape. | Take three **drops** of this sleep medication before going to sleep.
**telo** lili li lon poka tomo. tenpo mute la **waso** li lon ona. | There's a small pond by the house. There are **ducks** on it often.

eng: to lower one's body, often in order to hide
examples: pakala suli li kama la mi **len** e mi lon monsi supa. | When the explosion went off, I **ducked** behind a table.
mi pali ala **lon** tenpo pi pali ala. | I don't work **during** the break.
jan **ale** o open e lipu ona. | **Each** person should open their books.
kiwen wawa li pakala lili taso e selo pi **kute** ona. | The bullet only grazed his **ear**.

tok: lon open a, lon tenpo pi {ijo} ala
examples: mi kama lon tomo kulupu **lon tenpo ni**: jan ale ante li awen **lon ala**. | I arrived **early** at work.

eng: land (as opposed to sky or sea)
examples: jan li lawa e **ma** la sewi li lawa e sewi. | Whereas mortals rule the **Earth**, the divine rules over heaven.
**ko** ni **ma** li pona tawa kasi. | This **earth** is good for agriculture.

tok: nasin pi kama suno; see usage note
eng: the direction that the Earth rotates in around its axis
examples: la ona li tawa tan **nasin pi kama suno,** la ona li kama lukin e ma supa lon ma Sina. ona li awen lon ma ni. | And it came to pass, as they journeyed from **the east,** that they found a plain in the land of Shinar; and they dwelt there.

tok: wile e wawa lili, wile e pali lili
examples: pali nanpa wan li **wile e wawa lili**. pali nanpa pini li wile e wawa mute e tenpo mute. | The first task was **easy**. The final task was [difficult] and required a lot of time.
ona li **moku e** ijo jaki. | He's **eating** something nasty.

eng: [teach]ing or the knowledge gained from it
examples: kulupu lawa li **pana** ala e **sona** namako tawa mi. | I didn't receive higher **education**.

eng: the consequence or result of a cause
examples: tan li len. ale li ken lukin e **kama** ona. | The cause is hidden. The **effect** is visible to all.
kulupu pi pali lawa li **kama e** lawa sin lon tenpo sike pini. | The new law was **effected** by parliament last year.

eng: willpower or physical power required to do something
examples: sina wile kama nanpa wan lon lipu nimi, la ni li wile e **wawa** mute. | If you wanna win lexicography, that requires a lot of **effort.**

eng: the ball produced by a parent, often a bird
examples:
sina pali seme kepeken ko jelo pi **sike waso**? | What did you make with the **egg** yolk?
seme li kama lon open: mama sike anu **sike mama**? | What came first, the [chicken] or the **egg**?

tok: jan ni: ona li sona ala e {meli, mije, tonsi} ona
eng: person who doesn't know they are [transgender]
examples: tenpo weka la **mi sona ala e mije mi** li awen wile a sama mije pona pi kulupu mi. | I used to get so much gender envy from all the lovely guys in my friend group back when **I was an egg**.
sitelen **ale** li pona sama tawa mi. | I like **either** of the paintings as much as the other.

pana ona li ken pona wawa li ken ike musi. | His show will **either** be amazing or laughably bad.
o weka. sina ni ala la mi utala pakala e sina. | **Either** you leave on your own, or I'll kick your ass.

eng: physical phenomena related to the presence and flow of electric charge
examples: mi pilin e linja mi kepeken sike kon la, linja en sike li wile wan tan **wawa** awen. | When I rub a balloon on my hair, my hair and the balloon attract each other due to static **electricity**.
mi olin a e sina. ijo **ante** ala li suli sama tawa mi. | I love you like nothing **else**.
mi o ken pona e pakala. **ken ala la** ale pi pali mi li pini. | We've got to fix the problem, **or else** the whole project is going to end.

eng: to pay someone for their work
examples: mi **pali** tawa esun pan la ona li **pana e mani** tawa mi. | I'm **employed** at a bakery.
mi pini e pali **kepeken** nasin sin. | I **employed** a novel workflow to finish the task.
**pini** pi linja lawa li kama tu la o weka e ona. | When the **ends** of your hair start getting frizzy, it's time to cut them off.
ike la mi weka lon insa **tenpo** li ken ala lon pini. | I sadly had to leave in the middle of the event and didn't get to see the **end**.

mi **pilin pona tan** suno. | I'm **enjoying** the sun.
lipu li **pona** ala tawa mi. | I didn't **enjoy** that book.
ni o supa pi **mute pona**. | That should be **enough** chairs.

eng: the outer layer of skin on an animal (including humans)
examples: ken ala lukin mi li tan ala pakala kin pi **selo** mi. | Nor is my invisibility exactly a matter of a biochemical accident to my **epidermis**.
o sona: utala la kulupu kama en kulupu awen li **sama** ala. | We should not **equate** the occupied with the occupier.
pilin mi la sina suwi **namako** tawa mi lon tenpo poka. | It feels like you've been **especially** nice to me lately.
o kama e jan ale. **suli a la** jan Teno. | Make sure to invite everyone, **especially** Tenoch.
pali sina li **suli mute a** tawa pali mi. ona ala la mi ken ala pali. | Your work is **essential** to what I do. I couldn't work without it!

eng: substance used in feminizing hormone therapy
examples:
mi **moku** e **kiwen** lon tenpo mute la sijelo mi li kama **meli**. | My body is feminizing under the effects of **estrogen** pills.
tenpo poka la mi kama ken **moku meli**! | Soon, I'll be able to take **estrogen**!
mi pana e moku **pi mute sama** tawa jan ale. | I **evenly** distributed the food among the guests.
mi pana e mani luka tu wan e mani lili mute mute luka luka tawa ona. ni la **wile mani** ona **li pini**. | I gave them 7.50€. Now we're **even** again.
jan lawa **kin** li pilin ike tan moli ona. | **Even** the king was distraught by the loss.

len noka li li lili ike a. ona li len ala e insa noka **a**! | These socks are way too short. They don't **even** cover the knees!
suno suli sina li lon! mi pana e ijo tawa sina **a**. | It's your graduation day! I **even** brought you a gift.
**tenpo ale la** mi weka ala tan tomo mi. | I don't **ever** leave my house.
mi lukin e lipu **ale** lon ni. | I've read **every** book here.
**jan ale** o! o kalama ala! | **Everyone!** Be quiet!
**ijo ale** li ken lon. | **Everything** is possible.
mi sona ala kepeken nimi ni. o kepeken ona lon toki. mi o ken sona tan ni. | I don't understand how to use that word. Could you give an **example** in a sentence so I can see how it works?
o tawa lon suno laso taso. jan o lukin e **nasin pona sina** o wile sama. | Only walk when the light is green. Set a **good example** for others.

tenpo musi kalama li kama poka a. mi **wile a** lon ona! | The concert is happening soon. I'm so **excited** to go!!
nanpa li **musi wawa** tawa mi a. | I get really **excited** about math.

lon pi wile mi taso li pona. lon soweli pi wile ala li pona tawa ni. taso **lon** ala li pona nanpa wan. | It is good to be a cynic — it is better to be a contented cat — and it is best not to **exist** at all.

eng: the way out of a building
examples: seli li kama lon insa tomo la o **weka** kepeken **nasin** poka. | In case of a fire, leave through the nearest **exist**.
**sona** mi **la**, ona li kama lon tenpo poka. | I **expect** them to be here soon.

eng: to believe that something is required (of someone)
examples: jan lawa li **wile** e mute a tan mi. | My boss **expects** so much from me.
mi en sina li **lukin** e musi sin lon poka. ni li suli tawa mi. | I'm glad we **experienced** that movie together.
tenpo poka la mi o weka e soweli moli tan tomo mi. ni li jaki a tawa mi. | I had to get a dead rat out of my flat recently. It was a horrible **experience**.
sina **sona ala sona** pali lon ilo ni? | Do you have **experience** working this machine?
tenpo kulupu la mi **pana e sona** pi nasin moku kasi. | When class is in session, I'll **explain** how plants get nutrients.

eng: an organ through which animals see
examples: suno wawa li ike e pilin pi **lukin** mi. | Bright lights hurt my **eyes**.
mi tu e **len** li wan sin e ona la mi ken pali e len lawa musi. | If I cut **fabric** and sew it back together, I can make a funny hat.

eng: to turn one's face towards, looking at
examples: o **lukin** e suno. | **Face** the light.

eng: the front part, especially of a human head
examples: jan li utala e sina la sina kama ala kama sona e **sinpin** ona? | Did you remember the **face** of the person who attacked you?
mi wile ala **kama ike** tawa jan ale! | I don't want to **lose face** before everyone!

o pini e toki pilin. o open e toki pi **sona lon**. | Let's move on from opinions and consider the **facts**.
pilin sina la ona li jo e mani mute. taso **lon** la ona li jo e mani ala. | You thought he's rich, but **in fact** he's penniless.

eng: "the fact that ..." — a relative clause cliché
examples: toki Lusi li kepeken sitelen Kililisa. **ni** li ike e ken sona mi. | **The fact that** Russian uses Cyrillic makes it hard for me to read.
lipu kasi li **tawa anpa**. | The leaves are **falling**.
mi lukin e ona li **kama** olin. | I saw them and **fell** in love.

ma Loma nanpa tu li **pini** tan kulupu Tuki. | The second Rome **fell** to the Turks.
jan mute pona li **moli** lon ma utala. | Many good men **fell** in battle.

tok: tenpo pan, tenpo pi moli lipu
eng: autumn, season marked by falling leaves and harvest
examples:
**lipu kasi li moli la tenpo** ni li kama: sin la mi o tawa tomo sona. | As **fall** is upon us, once again I have to go to school.
lipu mun la **tenpo pan** li tenpo suli nanpa TW li kama lon mun nanpa LTT. | Going by the calendar, **fall** is the third season and starts in September.

eng: group of people based on kinship
examples: **kulupu mama** mi li wile tawa lon telo. taso mi wile ala. | My **family** want to travel by boat, but I don't want to join them.

ona li tawa ma **weka**. | She travelled to a **far** land.
tenpo kama **weka** la ken la sijelo jan li pakala ala e pilin ona. | In the **far** future, perhaps, diseases do not cause suffering.
mi kama sona e ni: ona li jo e mani mute **a**. | He turned out to be **far** richer than I'd thought.

eng: place where crops or livestock are raised
examples: lon monsi pi tomo mi la mi **mama** e kili moku lon insa **ma**. | In our backyard, we have a carrot **farm**.
mi moku **kepeken tenpo lili**. | I am a **fast** eater.

eng: same as 1, but emphasizing power needed to perform the task quickly
examples: sina pali **wawa** a!! | You worked **fast**!!
**mama** mi li weka tawa esun. | My **father**'s out to shop.

tok: pilin monsuta, pilin pi ike ken
examples: mi tawa lon pimeja la mi **pilin e ike ken** ni: kon li kama li moli e mi. | Walking in the dark, I'm always **afraid** that a ghost will come out and murder me.
suno la mi **pilin** pona, suno ante la pona ala. | Some days I feel well. On other days, not so much.
**pilin** mi **la** pali ni li ken ike a. | I **feel** that this is not a good idea.
ona li sama kiwen tawa lukin, taso mi **pilin** e ona la ona li ko. | I looked like it was hard, but when I **felt** it, it was like goo.
kiwen li seli **tawa luka**. | The stone **feels** hot.
sina wile kama jo e telo wawa la mani luka luka li mute ike a. | If you're buying an energy drink, ten dollars **feels** like too much.
mi lukin e jan lon ni. | I saw a **few** people there.
o pini! ona **pi mute lili** taso li lon. | Stop! There's only a **few** left.
**ma supa** suli li lon sinpin mi. | A large **field** lay before us.

eng: a place used to grow crops
examples: monsi tomo la mi **mama** e **pan** lon **ma**. | We have a wheat **field** in our backyard.
pali mi la mi alasa e sona sijelo sin. | I'm a scientist in the **field** of anatomy.
soweli mi li wile **utala** tan ni: ona wan li weka e moku tan ona ante. | My cats want to **fight** because one took food from the other.

poki telo ni li jo e **selo** lon insa. selo li awen e ona tan utala telo. | Aluminum cans have a **film** on the inside which protects them from the acidity of the drinks inside.
**lipu** pi kasi lili li lon sewi telo. | There is a **film** of algae on the pond.

eng: a sheet or strip of material used to take pictures or videos
examples: tenpo ni la, ilo sitelen li kepeken ala **lipu**. | Cameras these days don't use **film**.
soweli li suwi li lukin e mi! mi o **sitelen** e ni **lon ilo**! | Aww, the orangutan's so cute; it's looking at me! I should be **filming** this!

**pini** pi utala suli **la** tonsi li kama wawa. | After an arduous battle, nonbinary people will **finally** be empowered.
**pini la** mi pana e kili tawa sewi. | **Finally**, I placed a cherry on top.

eng: (indicates exasperation); after a lot of struggle
examples:
tawa suli li pini. mi kama **a** lon tomo mi. | The long journey is over. We're **finally** home.
**pona a**! ijo sin li kama! | **Finally**, a brand new update!

tok: kama lukin e, kama kute e, etc
eng: to come across something after searching for it
examples: mi alasa mute e soweli la mi **kama lukin** e ona. | After searching for the beast, I finally **found** it.

eng: to opine, often based on past experiences
examples: mi **pilin e ni:** jan li wile a utala. mi sona e ni tan pali mi. | I've **found** that people actually WANT to fight. I know this because of my job.
jan wan pi pali mani mi li **pona mute tawa lukin**, taso mi o olin ala e ona. | One of my co-workers looks so **fine**, but I need to not fall for him.

eng: charge money due to legal infraction
examples: mi tawa pi wawa ike e ilo mi la jan lawa li kama li toki e ni: mi **o pana e mani**. | I was speeding in my car, and a cop came and **fined** me for it.
mi wile sitelen lon moku suwi la mi o tawa **pona** e luka. | Writing on this cake requires **fine** movements.
mi tawa anpa lon nasin nena, taso mi **pona**. | I fell on the hike up the hill, but I'm **fine**.
mi wile tu e kili la mi pakala e **luka** mi. | I tried to cut tomatoes and ended up cutting my **finger**.
o tawa tomo mi lon tenpo pimeja ni! poka tomo la mi pali e **seli** suli li pali e moku lon ni. | Come to my house tonight! We'll make a big **fire** and cook on it.

eng: coming before all others in a list
examples: kulupu lipu Tanaka la lipu **nanpa wan** li toki e open ale. | The **first** book of the Tanakh talks about the genesis of the world.
taso **open** la kulupu li pana e mani tawa mi. mi o toki e ona. | But **first**, let me tell you about my sponsor.

eng: a water-dwelling animal or its flesh
examples:
tenpo sike pini MLL la poka pi ma Kanata la jan pi mute ike li alasa e **kala** la kala li pini. | In the 1990s the **fish** stocks in Canadian waters depleted due to overfishing.
tenpo ni la jan o moku ala e soweli e waso. taso ona li awen ken moku e **kala**. | During this period people should not consume meat. **Fish**, however, is still allowed.
jan pi tomo mi li pali e pan lili mute. mi moku e ona **luka**. | My roommate made 20 cookies. I ate **five** of them.
o **pona** e ilo tenpo mi **pakala**. | Please **fix** my watch.
mi awen ala **wile** lukin e ma lon suno ni. | I've gotten my **fix** on touristing for the day.

eng: a piece of cloth used as a symbol
examples: **lipu** pi ma Sonko la mun jelo luka li lon. | The **flag** of China has five yellow stars.

eng: to mark as important with the intention to resolve later
examples:
ona li **suli** e pakala mi la, mi pona e ona. | They **flagged** my mistake, so I fixed it.
o **loje** e lipu tan kulupu pi linja toki. | **Flag** that email from the phone company.

eng: the bottom surface in a room
examples: pakala! telo mi li tawa **supa** tan poki a. | Crap! My drink spilled onto the floor.
mi kulupu e **kasi** pi **kule** mute li pana e ona tawa olin mi. | I collected **flowers** in many colors and brought them to my boyfriend.
mi esun e kili palisa la **pipi** kili mute li kama lon tomo mi... | When I bought bananas, a bunch of fruit **flies** came home with them...

tok: tawa (lon) kon, tawa (lon) sewi, (tawa sama) waso
examples: sina pana e sike la ona li **tawa** mi **lon kon**. | You threw the frisbee and it **flew** towards me.

mi wile **tawa** olin mi la mi o **kepeken waso**. | If I want to visit my partner, I have to **fly**.
ona li awen **kepeken** ilo ni: ona la ona li kama sona e nasin tawa pi ilo waso. | She's still **flying** the plane she trained with when she was first learning.
nasin ike sina li **ken** ala lon tomo ni. | This kind of behavior won't **fly** here.
musi Osu en musi sama li wile e **wawa lawa** mute. | Games like osu! require a lot of **focus**.

eng: to perceive strongly or solely, often for an extended duration
examples:
mi ken ala **kute pona** e toki sina. lawa mi li ken ala weka e kalama poka pi toki ala. | I'm struggling to **focus** on your speech. My brain can't filter out the background noise.
mi ken ala **awen lukin** e lipu lon tenpo suli. lawa mi li kama pilin e ijo ante. | I can't **focus** on reading for a long time. I always get distracted.

eng: to bend a sheet in order to make it compact
examples:
o **lili** e len o poki e ona. | **Fold up** the bedsheets and put them in the drawer.
mi open e lipu ona, li lukin e toki ona ale, li **pini** sin e ona. | I unfolded their letter, read what it said, and **folded** it back **up**.

eng: take the same route as another
examples: sina o lawa e kulupu. mi **tawa sama** sina. | You lead the group. I'll **follow**

eng: have a certain lifestyle or doctrine
examples: mi **lon** nasin Pawaki. | I **follow** the Baháʼí faith.

eng: on social media, subscribe to someone's posts
examples: sina **kute ala kute** e toki ale mi? | Are you **following** me?
sijelo ale li wile e **moku**. | All organisms need **food** to survive.
sitelen la **kili** en **kasi** mute li lon. | The picture showed various vegetarian **foods**.

eng: an organ used by animals to support oneself on the ground
examples: mi tawa pi mute ike la **noka** mi li kama pakala. | I ran too much, so my **foot** was injured.

eng: a unit of measurement equal to 12 inches, most commonly used in the United States
examples: nanpa pi ma Juwese la, suli mi li sama suli noka luka wan. | (In American units) I am about six **feet* tall.

eng: to [pay] for something, especially if the cost is high
examples: sina tu li ken ala la mi ken **pana e mani**. | If you two can't, I can **foot the bill**.

mi pali e pan suwi **tawa** sina. | I made cookies **for** you.
o **tawa** nena ma! | Run **for** the hills!
ona li pona tawa mi **tan** ijo mute. | I like her **for** lots of reasons.

suno tu a **la** mi awen lon ni. | We've been waiting here **for** two days.
mi len e mi lon **lon** tenpo suli. | I've been masking my true self **for** a long time.
jan luka la **pona**. jan tu la pona ala. | Ten people voted **for**, two against.

eng: providing an instance demonstrating a pattern or rule
examples: sina wile toki e ken la, nimi 'sama' li ken pona, sama ni: "nimi 'sama' li ken pona, sama ni." | If you want to discuss possibilities, the word 'sama' may be effective; for example, "nimi 'sama' li ken pona, sama ni."

ona li lon tomo lili lon telo suli la ona li ken a pilin e **wawa** ona. | Being in a small boat on a great ocean, she really felt the **force** of the water.
**wawa** li wawa ale, ala li ken pini e ona. sinpin li wawa ale li ken pini e ale. ona li kama wan la seme li kama? | Who would win, an unstoppable **force** or an immovable object?

eng: make someone act against their will
examples: mi pana e moku tawa jan mi **lon wile ala ona**. | I **forced** my kid to eat.

tok: lon tenpo ale (kama), tawa tenpo ale (kama)
examples: o moku e pan **lon tenpo ale kama.** | Eat bread **forever.**

mi **weka e sona** mute pi tenpo ni: mi jan lili. | I **forget** a lot of my childhood. 
sina **weka** ala **weka** e ilo sina lon poka pi poki telo suli? | Did you **forget** your phone at the poolside? 
mi **kama sona ala** e ni: mi lon ni tan seme. | I **forgot** why I came here. 
tenpo pini la jan Nansi li sona pali e moku ante mute. tenpo ni la ona li **sona ala**. | Previously, Nancy knew how to prepare many different dishes. Now, she has **forgotten**.
mi **kama ala** pana e namako tawa kala. | I **forgot** to add salt to the fish.

eng: a period of 14 days and nights
examples: mi awen lon **suno luka luka tu tu,** taso akesi wawa li kama sin ala. | I waited for a **fortnight,** but the dragon didn't return.

jan **tu tu** li lon kulupu olin mi. | **Four** people are in my friend group.
mi **tu tu** e pan. | I **cut** the cake **twice** (into **four** pieces).
kulupu Palasina o **ken ale**! | **Free** Palestine!

tok: ken, ken ale, ala li weka e ken mi
examples: mi pali e pan la mi ken pana e suwi pi mute wile tawa ona. mi jan **pi ken ale**. | I can put as much sugar as I want in my cookies. I'm a **free** woman.

sijelo jan li kama pakala la ma Kanata la ni li **wile ala e mani**: jan li tawa tomo sijelo la jan sona li pona e sijelo ona. | If you're injured in Canada, it's **free** to go to a hospital and be treated by a doctor.

eng: a receptacle that keeps food items cold, but above freezing
examples: **poki lete** mi li kama e pilin ike wawa lon mi tan ni: lon monsi poki, la poki lili li lon. mi sona ala e insa. | My **fridge** scares me a lot because in the back, there are some small containers. I don't know what's inside them.

eng: someone physically and emotionally close and enjoyable
examples:
jan Sankin li pona tawa mi. ike la mi tu li **poka** ala. | I like John Green, but unfortunately we are not **friends**.
ona li pona e mi lon tenpo utala. mi tu li awen **pona**. | He helped me out during the war, and we've remained **friends** since.

eng: a nonspecific person from one's social circle
examples:
ni la mi kama pilin e ijo. tenpo poka la **jan** li toki e ijo musi tawa mi. | That reminds me of a funny story a **friend** told me recently.
mi en **jan** tu **ante** li wile lukin e sitelen la mi esun e lipu ken tawa ni. | I bought movie tickets for myself and two **friends**.

eng: in the plural: a friend group, a clique
examples: len mi sin li ike tawa **kulupu** mi. | My **friends** think my new jeans are weird.
**akesi** li moku e pipi. | The **frog** eats bugs.

mi kama **tan** ma Mewika. | I came **from** America.
jan mute li moli **tan** ike sijelo. | Many people die **from** disease.

pilin mi **en** pilin sina li sama ala. | You opinions differ **from** mine.
ona li sona **e** ike **e** pona. | He knows right **from** wrong.

tomo kama la tenpo kama li weka suli. — **ike a**. | The next bus only comes in 30 minutes. — **Fuck**.
**aaaaa**, noka mi a!! | **Fuck**, my toe!!
a! moku ni li wawa **a**! | Oh! **Fuck**, this stuff tastes good!
kulupu la mi o len nasa ala. taso **ona li ike**. mi kute ala. | The guys at work keep saying I dress weird. But **fuck them**, I'll do whatever I want.
mi en ona li **unpa** lon ale pi tenpo pimeja. | Me and them were **fucking** all night.

tok: ale ken li lon, ijo (sin) ala li ken
eng: having little to no empty space
examples: poki pan la **pan ale ken li lon**. | **All the bread that will fit is in** the breadbox.

tok: wile ala moku (sin), ken ala moku
examples: "sina wile ala wile moku e suwi?" ni ala; mi **ken ala moku**. | "Do you want to eat dessert?" No, I'm **full**.

eng: any organism of the Fungi kingdom
examples: mi pana e telo kili tawa tomo pimeja li weka tan tomo lon tenpo suli, la mi pilin e ni: mi kama sin, la ma **soko** wawa li lon. | If I leave fruit juice in a dark room and leave the room for a long period of time, I think I'll return to a beautiful **fungus** garden.
jan mute a li kama lukin e sitelen **musi** soweli. | The **funny** cat video went viral.
telo li pana e kon pi jaki **nasa**. | The milk smelt **funny**.
kulupu suli pi **jan soweli** li kama lon ma mi. | There's a large **furry** convention happening in my city.

eng: a time yet to be experienced
examples: **tenpo kama** la mi wile kama sona e toki mute. | In the **future**, I want to learn many languages.
pali pi jan lon li weka e ken pi jan pi **tenpo kama**. | The actions of people of the present are harming the opportunities of **future** generations.
sina **kama jo** e jan ni: ona li wile pona tawa sina. | You've **gained** a friend.

tok: sijelo, luka, noka, monsi, etc; pona, wawa, suli
examples: o lukin e **luka wawa** ni a!! | Look at these bicep **gains!!**

tok: kama lon poka, kama poka lon monsi
eng: to follow closely behind and close the gap over time
examples: ona li **kama poka lon monsi** mi la mi suli e wawa tawa. | When she started **gaining on** me, I ran faster.
mi wile **musi** supa lon poka sina tan ni: sina suwi. | I want to play a board **game** with you because you're so sweet.

eng: to play with enjoyable programs on computers
examples: mi wile **musi** mute **lon ilo,** taso mama li ken ala e ni. | I want to **game** a lot, but my parents don't allow it.

eng: to mess with or exploit a system
examples: mi **nasa** e nasin mani pi ma Tosi. | I'm **gaming** the German bank system.

tok: jan musi, jan pi musi ilo
eng: someone who plays enjoyable programs on computers
examples: mi toki ala tawa **jan pi musi ilo** tan ni: mi lili, la musi ilo li moli e mama mama mi. | I don't talk to **gamers** because gaming killed my grandma.

tok: olin e {meli/mije}, olin e kule sama
examples:
ona li mije li **olin e mije**. | He's a guy and he's **gay**.
jan mute li kama kulupu. ona ale li **olin e kule sama**. | Many people gathered. They're all **gay**.

eng: social category of man, woman, nonbinary, etc.
examples:
**mije en meli la** mi **poki** ala. | I don't fit into the **gender binary**.
**mije la meli la tonsi la** ale o kama e mani sama. | People deserve the same welfare regardless of **gender**.

eng: involving many or all members of a group
examples: nasin sin li pona e jan **mute**. | The new policy improved **general** quality of life.
mi en jan olin mi li **kama jo** e soweli lili suwi a! | Me and my partner **got** a pet!
sina **jo** ala **jo** e lipu ken? | Have you **got** a ticket?
o **kama** e supa tan tomo ante. jan kama li wile e ona. | Please **get** a chair from the other room for our guest.

mi **kama** wile moku. | I'm **getting** hungry.
ona li **kama** pona tawa mi. | I'm **getting** to like him.
mi **sona** a. | Yeah, I **get** it.
a, kulupu li pakala a e sina lon utala! | Whoa, you really **got** wrecked in the fight!

tok: o kama pona; or many others
examples: jaki li lon sijelo mi la mi wawa ala. — ike a! **tenpo jaki sina o lili taso**! | I'm sick and have no energy. — Oh no! **I hope you won't stay sick for long**!

eng: spirit of a dead person, sometimes considered frightening
examples:
pilin mi la jan li **moli** la ona li kama ala **kon**. | I don't believe in **ghosts**.
sinpin sina li kama walo a! sina lukin e **jan moli** anu seme? | Your face is so pale! Did you see a **ghost**?

eng: an object given without a required reciprocation
examples:
a, **pana** mi li ike tawa sina. mi o weka e ona e mi tan poka sina. mi o awen weka tawa tenpo ale. | Oh, you didn't like my **gift.** I should take it back and leave your side. I should stay away forever.
mama mi li sike sin e suno la mi alasa **pana** e **ijo** tawa ona. | I'm looking for a birthday **gift** for my mom.
**meli** li pona la ona li kama lon ma sewi ike lon moli ona. | All the good **girls** go to hell.

mi **pana** e moku e olin tawa soweli mi. | I **gave** my cat his food and my love.
tenpo pana la mi **pana** e kiwen namako kute tawa jan olin mi. | For Christmas I **gave** my partner earrings.

mi uta suwi e lawa soweli. | I **gave** the kitten a kiss on the forehead.
sina wile ala wile ken e mi? | Would you like to **give** me permission?
mi **lukin lili** taso e kulupu la mi awen ala sona e sinpin jan. | I only **glanced** at the group, so I don't remember their faces.

tok: kiwen, kiwen ni: sina ken lukin e ijo pi monsi ona
eng: a hard substance that is transparent or transluscent
examples: lupa pi tomo mi la **kiwen ni** li lon: jan li lukin lon ona la ona li ken lukin e ijo pi monsi ona. | My window has a glass pane in it.

eng: move along a surface without being stopped by friction
examples:
ilo tawa li **tawa** pona e tomo lon sewi telo. | The propeller allowed the boat to **glide** smoothly across the water.
ona li **tawa** musi lon supa lete. | They were **gliding** giddily on the ice surface.
mi pana e lipu **tawa kon** li lukin e **tawa** ona. | We threw paper planes and watched them **glide**.
sitelen ni li lon pimeja la ona li **suno** a. | That charm can **glow** in the dark.
mi lukin e **suno** kule lon ma pimeja pi monsi tomo. | I could see a neon **glow** from the woods behind the backyard.

eng: a wet material that can bind two objects together once dried
examples: **ko** ni li ken **wan** wawa e ale! | This **glue** can fix anything!
mi pakala e suno tomo, taso mi ken **wan** sin e anpa e sewi **kepeken ko**! | I broke my chandelier, but I can **glue** the bottom and the top together!
tomo ni li **tawa** ma Sinsinasi. | This train is **going** to Cincinnati.

o **weka** ala tan mi. | I don't want you to **go**.
mi **tawa**. | I'm **going**.
pakala la ilo li **pali** ala! | The engine just won't **go**!
mi wile pona e tomo mi. mi **kama** ni a. | I wanted to clean my room. I'll **go** do that now.

eng: a horned mammal that lives in the mountains and is known for ramming into things
examples: mi weka e palisa tan lawa **soweli** li kalama kepeken ona. | I severed a horn from the **goat's** head and I'm using it to make noise.

tok: jan wawa, jan suli, jan sewi
eng: "greatest of all time", a particularly revered or appreciated person
