examples: sina taso li ken e ni: mi lon tomo ni. sina **wawa mute a**. | Thank you for helping me get into the club. You're **the goat**.
open la **sewi** li lon e sewi e ma. | In the beginning **God** created the heavens and the Earth.

eng: the divine characterised in terms of personhood
examples: ma Elena pi tenpo weka la jan li toki mute e **jan sewi** ona. | Ancient Greek texts share lots of stories about their **gods**.

pona **a** la mi kama ken lape! | Oh my **god**, finally I can sleep!
sina tawa lon ma pi ilo moli tan seme **a**? | **God**, what were you thinking just walking across the shooting range?
seme li kama? | What's **going** to happen?

mi lon ma nena la mi kama jo e **kiwen mani**. | We found a chunk of **gold** during our hike in the mountains.
kiwen walo li kama mani lili la **kiwen jelo** li awen mani suli. | While the price of silver dropped, the price of **gold** stayed high.
lipu kule pi ma Wenesa la soweli **jelo** li lon. | The flag of Venice features a **gold** lion.

mama o, jan ale li moli. | Mama, we're all **gonna** die.
mi **wile** esun e ilo pi mani lili. | I'm **going to** buy a computer for cheap.
mi weka lon suno kama nanpa tu. | I'm **gonna** leave in two days.
mi **kama** pali e moku a. | I'm **gonna** cook something now.

eng: ethically moral, pleasant, effective, proper, reasonable, etc.
examples: o **pona** tawa jan. jan o **pona** tawa sina. | Do **good** to others and others will do **good** to you.
a! pimeja li kama suli! **mi weka**. | Ah! It's gotten late! **Good night**!
**o lape pona**, jan lili suwi o. | **Good night**, kiddo.

"o sona: nasin mi la sina ken pali ale wile-" "a, pakala, jan, a, pi tomo mi li wile e mi, **mi weka."** | "As you can see, with my method you can do anything you want-" "Oh, fuck, someone, uh, in my house wants me, **goodbye."**

tok: tawa pona, o tawa pona, weka pona, o weka pona
eng: (farewell said to someone who is leaving)
examples: a sina tawa. **o tawa pona** | Oh, you need to go. **Goodbye**.
esun pi mani lili la mi **kama jo** e ilo. | I **got** a computer from the discount store.
mi **jo** e ilo moli. meli ala. meli ale o moli. | I **got** a gun, no girls, girls gotta die.
jan majuna li awen **lawa** e ma ni lon tenpo suli. | The old man has **governed** this land for decades.

eng: an organisation that rules a state
examples: pilin pi jan mute la **lawa ma** li pali sama ala wile mi. | According to the people, the **government** is not doing what we want it to.
lupa telo **suli** li lon ma Ewisona. | The **Grand** Canyon is in Arizona.
o esun e pan tawa moku kama. sina ni la **pona**. | If you could buy some bread for dinner, that would be **grand**.
mi wan e kiwen sinpin pimeja kepeken ko **walo**. | The black tiles are held together by a **grey** paste.
linja mute ona li kama walo a. taso linja lili li awen **pimeja**. | Most of her hair is white by now, but a couple of hairs are still **gray**.
jan **wawa** Kolija li kama. | Goliath the **great** is coming.
soweli mi li moli ala. ni li **pona.** | My cat didn't die. That's **great.**

kiwen **suli** li pini e open lupa. | A **great** boulder blocked the opening to the cave. 
pilin pi ike **mute** li kama lon mi. | A **great** sadness has washed over me.

eng: expression of approval and strong positive emotions
examples: sina pali pona. **wawa a!** | You've done good work. **Great!**

eng: a shade of turquoise/grue; the color of many plants
examples: open sike la, kasi li kama **laso** sin. | At the beginning of the year, plants turn **green** again.

eng: a shade of turquoise/grue as opposed to [blue]
examples: nasa la telo ni li **laso** sama **kasi**. | Strangely, this water is green.

eng: having a positive impact on the environment
examples: ilo mute sin li **pona tawa ma**, taso kulupu mute li pana ala e mani tawa ona. | Lots of new technologies are **green**, but many organizations don't invest in them.
ilo waso li tawa anpa li kama lon **ma**. | The plane flew down and hit the **ground**.
kulupu pi tomo moku o pali wawa e moku mute tawa **kulupu** mi suli. | The restaurant staff had to work hard accommodate our large **group**.

kasi linja li **kama suli** li selo e ale tomo. | The ivy **grew** to envelop the whole house.
wile moku mi li awen **kama suli**. | My hunger is only **growing**.
mi o jo ala e **ilo moli** tan ni: jo la mi moli e mi. | I don't think a **gun** is personally right for me because I would kill myself.
poka tomo la **jan** li lon li esun e ijo. | There was this **guy** next to the building selling some knick-knacks.
mi **mije**. awen la mi kule suwi meli e sinpin mi lon tenpo. | I'm a **guy**, but I like to wear makeup regardless.

eng: filaments that grow out from scalp
examples: **linja** mi li nasa jaki la o lukin ala e mi. | My **hair** is unkempt, so avert your gaze.

eng: filaments that grow out of skin
examples: tonsi la pali mije pi ante sijelo li ken suli e **linja sijelo** sina. | Undergoing masculine gender transition may thicken your body **hair**.
jan ale pi musi Geometry Dash o sewi e **luka** sina a! | All the GD players, put your **hands** up!

eng: (in card games) set of cards in one's possession
examples: ona li len e **lipu** ona, taso uta ona pi pilin pona li toki e ni: lipu li wawa mute a. | He's concealing his **hand**, but his grin betrays that his cards are really powerful.
mi **kin** li **pali** lon ni: sitelen li kama toki Tuki. | I had a **hand** in creating the show's Turkish dub.

eng: to kill someone with a noose
examples: jan li pali ike lon ma ni la jan ni li kama **moli** tan **linja.** | If someone breaks the law here, they will be **hanged.**

eng: to place an object such that it is supported from above
examples: mi **pana** e len mi tawa sinpin tomo. | I'll **hang** my coat on the wall.

eng: an understanding or familiarity (to get the hang of)
examples: mi **kama sona** e nasin pona pi lawa ilo. | I'm **getting the hang** of driving.

eng: to be supported from above (usually by a taught object)
examples: sijelo soweli li **lon linja** tan sewi. | The meat is **hung from** the roof.
**o awen.** mi wile lukin e lipu ni. | **Hold on.** I want to read this flier.

mi **lon kulupu** poka mi. | I'm **hanging out** with my friends. 
jan lili pi tenpo ni li wile **lon poka** taso li wile ala esun e nanpa a! | Kids these days just wanna **hang out** instead of trading stocks!

seme a!? seme li **kama**! | Help! What is **happening**?
ijo ike li **kama** lon mi la mi ken e ona li utala ala. | When something bad **happens**, I let it **happen** and don't put up a fight.

pona la mi lon ma Nujo sama sina. mi tu o kama wan a. | I **happen** to be in New York as well. Let's meet up!
len pi weka telo li lon ala lon sina? | Do you **happen** to have a tissue?
sina lon ala poka la mi ken ala **pilin pona** anu seme? | Is there really no **happiness** without this feeling?
jan Ilasa li kama nanpa wan lon utala pi nasin linja, la ona li **pilin pona.** | When Eliza won first place in the crochet competition, she was **happy.**

eng: (stock phrase said to someone whose [birthday] it is)
examples: **suno suli sina a**!! o musi pona! | **Happy birthday**!! Have a great time!
pan ni li kama **kiwen** la mi wile ala moku e ona. | This bread has gone **hard**, so I don't want to have it.

utala sona li **wile e pali** sona **mute** a. | One had to study **hard** for the exams.
mi tawa e kiwen la ni li **pali** pi **suli** ike a. | Moving rocks around is too **hard**.
o moku ala e kasi nasa **wawa**. ona li ken moli e sina. | Stay away from **hard** drugs, they are deadly.
mi **wile unpa,** taso mije suwi li lon ala tawa mi... | I'm **hard,** but there are no twinks for me...

tenpo ale la mi weka ala e **len lawa** mi. | I never remove my **hat**.
a, ni li **len** sina anu seme? | Oh, is this your **hat**?
kulupu sina li **ike wawa** tawa mi. ona o awen weka tan mi a! | I **hate** your clique's guts. Stay away from me!

mi **jo** e tomo e soweli. | I **have** a house and a dog.
sina **jo** ala **jo** e ilo open? | Do you **have** the key?
poki la len mute li **lon**. | The drawer **has** a lot of clothes.
ilo lili li **lon** mi. | I **have** a mobile phone.
mi **lawa** e tomo lili lon ma Sanpansiko. | I **have** an apartment in San Francisco.

mi telo e mi li moku. | I **had** a bath and then **had** dinner.
mi ken ala ken lukin e ni? | Can I **have** a look at that?

open suno la mi **o lon** tomo sona. ni la mi o kama lape. | I **have** classes in the morning, so I have to go to sleep.
suno kama nanpa tu wan la jan li lukin e luka mi pakala. | I **have** a doctor's appointment for my injured wrist in three days.

lawa mi li pakala. | I'm **having** a headache.
suno o pona! | **Have** a nice day!
ijo pilin li wile awen pilin. | Living beings **have** a will to live.
mi **o** tawa. | Sorry, I **have to** go.
mi en olin mi li **kama mama** e jan! | Me and my partner are **having** a kid!
sina o. lon la tenpo ni li **musi** ala a tawa mi. | Bro, I'm straight-up not **having a good time** right now.
mi o pali. taso mi awen wile **musi**. | I should work, but I continue to want to **have fun**.
**ona** li wile ala musi sike. | **He** doesn't want to play ball.
meli li uta suwi e **mije**. | She tenderly kissed **him**.
monsuta li weka e **lawa** ona li pana e ona sama ilo utala. | The monster ripped off its **head** and threw it like a missile.

eng: top or head-like end of a non-living thing
examples: mi weka e **pini** kasi. | I'm cutting off the flowers' **heads**.
ona li **unpa** e mi kepeken **uta**. | He gave me **head**.

eng: (in toki pona) the word used before a proper name, usually indicating the type or nature of the named object
examples:
**nimi open** pi nimi jan li suli tan seme? | Why is the **headnoun** capitalised?
sina kepeken **nimi lawa** seme? | Which **headnoun** do you use?
nimi "soweli Una" la, **nimi open** li nimi "soweli". | In the name "soweli Una", the **headnoun** is "soweli".

tok: ilo kute, ilo pi kute musi, ilo kalama kute
eng: a device or pair of devices placed on the ear used to listen to sounds privately
examples:
jan Sita o, **ilo kute** sina li awen lon tomo mi. | Sita, you left your **headphones** at my house.
ijo seme sina li pona nanpa wan tawa sina? **ilo** mi **pi kute musi**. | What's your favorite thing of yours? My **headphones**.
o pona tawa jan ante lon tomo ni. **ilo kalama** o lon **kute**. | Please be considerate of other passengers. Use **headphones** instead of speakers.

jan li moku e kon nasa la ni li **pona** ala tawa **sijelo**. | Smoking isn't good for your **health**.
**sijelo** pi mama mi li kama ike lon tenpo poka. | My dad's been in poor **health** lately.

mi **kute** e mu suwi soweli. | I **heard** the cat purring with content.
mi ken ala **kute** e sina! | I can't **hear** you!
mi pilin e sinpin sijelo la mi ken pilin e ni: **pilin insa** li tawa e telo. | When you touch your chest, you can feel your **heart** pumping blood.

sina sona pona e olin, jan lili o. taso, sina ken ala ken awen lon utala? | You have a kind **heart**, little one. But, can you endure in battle too?
jan Epinisa Su li weka e **nasin** ike ona li kama pana e olin tawa kulupu ona. | Ebenezer Scrooge had a change of **heart** and started showing love to those around him.
o lukin a! jan ni li sitelen e jan olin! **sitelen olin** suli li lon monsi ona a! olin ona li nanpa wan tawa mi a. | Aw, look, someone drew my favorite ship with a big **heart** behind them!

eng: thermal energy or the sensation caused by it
examples: sijelo sina li kama **seli** tan jaki la o awen lon supa lape o moku mute e telo. | When your body **heat** is high due to sickness, stay in bed and drink a lot.

eng: acknowledging presence, especially if intending to converse
examples: **toki** a, ale o! | **Hello**, everyone!

eng: acknowledging presence without intending to converse
examples: jan sin li kama lon musi. jan musi li **mu** tawa ona. | Another player joined the game. Players said **hello** to them.

tok: pona, pana e {pona, sona, ...}, pali (tawa pali mi)
examples: mi taso ala li pali e lipu. jan Lu kin li **pali wawa**. | I didn't write the book alone. Luz also **helped a lot**.
mi pana e ko tawa **ona**. ona o moku kepeken palisa. | I gave **her** a pudding to eat with a fork.
mi **lon**! | I'm **here**!
mi kama awen lon **ma ni** lon sike pini poka. | We moved **here** last year.

jan Pasi **o**! sina lon ma ni a tan seme! | **Hey** Fadi! How come you're here of all places!
**sina o**. mi alasa mute e pilin mi sina li wile kama toki lon e ona. | **Hey**, I've been thinking a lot on how I feel about you and I just want to be honest with you.
soweli suli li **lape** lon tenpo suli **tan lete**. | Bears **hibernate** in the winter.

eng: (of an electronic device) to enter a low-power state while saving data
examples: **ilo** mi li **lape lili** la pini lape ona li wile e tenpo lili. | Whenever my laptop **hibernates**, it takes a little bit of time to kick back in.
waso ni li **sewi** weka a! | That bird is really **high** up!
selo pi ma ni li **suli** mute la jan li ken ala tawa sewi ona. | The wall around this place is very high, so people can't go over it.
tomo ni la, toki pi lawa **sewi** o kama lon. | In this establishment, the words of the **high** officials are absolute.
ona li nena e ko walo pi mute ike li kama **nasa** a. | He snorted too much white powder and got really **high**.

eng: euphoric, as if having taken drugs
examples: ona li toki pona e sijelo mi. mi alasa e **pilin sewi** sama tan tenpo ni. | They complimented my body. I've been chasing that **high** ever since.

ma musi la **nena lili** mute li lon. sina ken open lon sewi **nena** li ken tawa anpa **nena.** musi mute. | At the playground there are lots of **hills**. You can start at the top of the **hill** and roll down to the bottom of the **hill.** It's a lot of fun.

mi olin suli e **ona**. | I love **him** so much.
sin la **ona** li toki tawa mi. | I heard back from **him** again.
jan Mewi li pilin pona tawa **ona**. | Mary likes **himself**.
tenpo pini mute la mu **ona** la mi kama wile utala. | Historically, **his** hiss has made me throw a hissy.

eng: events that happened in the past, collectively
examples:
**ijo ale pi tenpo pini** li kama sin lon sike. | **History** always repeats itself.
mi lukin e sitelen. ona li toki e **tenpo ale ni**: jan li alasa kama lon pini pi musi Malijo kepeken tenpo pi lili nanpa wan. | I'm watching a video on the **history** of Mario speedruns.

eng: to hurt or damage by striking
examples: mi utala la jan li kama **pakala luka** e mi! | We were arguing and he just **hit** me!

eng: to come into contact with, with a lot of force
examples: supa li **kama** lon anpa la ona li kalama li pakala wawa. | When the plate **hit** the ground, it made a crashing noise and shattered.
mi **jo** wawa ala e palisa kasi tan ni: ona li ken pakala. | I didn't **hold** on to the branch with force for fear that it might snap.
poki luka wan **li lon** poki suli. | The package **holds** six bottles.
o **awen** e telo mi. | **Hold** my beer.
toki mi pi tenpo pini li **awen lon**. | My claims from last year still **hold**.
**pilin** ona la ma musi li ike tan kalama ona tan suno ona. | He **holds** that the fun fair only produces unpleasant noise and bright lights.
tenpo open li **kama** lon suno kama. | The opening ceremony will be **held** tomorrow.

eng: a hollow opening in a surface; a blind hole
examples: mi **lupa** e ma la mi ken mama e kasi sin. | I dug a **hole** in the ground to grow a new plant.

eng: an opening that goes from one side to another of a surface; a through hole
examples: a, len noka mi la **lupa li** lon. | Huh, there's a hole in my sock.
jan Jowane Kutenpe la jan mute li kama ken lukin e lipu **sewi**. | Thanks to Johannes Gutenberg, many people got the opportunity to read the **holy** texts.

**tomo** mi li mani mute ala. awen la ona li pana e pilin suwi. | My **home** isn't very fancy. But it still gives me a homely feeling.
mi lon ala **tomo** mi. | I'm not **at home** right now.

tok: (toki) lon; len ala e wile lon
eng: expressing one's true feelings or intentions instead of concealing them
examples:
mi wile **toki lon** e pilin. | I want to be **honest** with you.
**ala** la mi **len e wile** mi **lon**. | I'm an **honest** man.
pilin mi **lon** li ni: mi ijo musi ala li awen taso tan kama pi moli mi. | **Honestly**, it feels like I'm just waiting to die.

eng: wish (for a good thing to happen)
examples:
noka sina pakala **o** kama pona! | **I hope** your broken leg gets better!
ona **o** ike sin sama ala. | **I hope** she doesn't pull any more bullshit like that.

eng: confidence that something good can happen
examples: mi **pilin a** e ni: mi awen alasa la mi **ken a** lukin sin e soweli mi. | I still have **hope** that we **can** find our cat again.

eng: large mammal which humans ride on for travel or recreation
examples: tenpo musi la mi tawa ma soweli li tawa lon monsi **soweli**! | During the vacation, we went to a pony farm and rode on **horse**back!

tok: tomo misiskeke, tomo pi kama pona
eng: a large institution of medical professionals that provides medical care
examples: noka mi li pakala la mi lon **tomo misikeke**. | My leg is broken so I need to stay at the **hospital**.
telo li awen **seli**. mi ken ala moku e ona. | The tea is still **hot**. I can't drink it.
tan seme a la tonsi **suwi** ala li lon supa mi!? | How can it be that there are no **hot** enbies in my bed right now!?

eng: a unit of time, 24 of which make up a day
examples: ale o pini e pali kepeken **tenpo suli** wan kepeken tenpo lili luka luka luka. | Everyone must finish the test within one **hour** and fifteen minutes.

eng: a dwelling or domicile, usually with walls and a roof
examples: **tomo** mi la sina ken ala kama lukin e ijo nasa. | In my **house** you won't be able to find any drugs.

eng: in what manner, by what means
examples:
mi ken pini e musi ni **kepeken nasin seme**? | **How** do we solve this puzzle?
mi ken kama pona sin **kepeken seme**? | **How** can I get better again?
ilo li seli **seme**? | **How** hot is the oven?
**tan seme** la pilin sina la mi o sona e ni? | **How** should I know?
suwi **a**! | **How** sweet!
jan Aso o! sina lon la pona a! **sina seme** lon tenpo poka! | Hey Ashot! It's good to see you! **How have you been** lately?

eng: an acknowledgement of someone's presence (e.g. passing by someone on a hallway), without intent for further conversation
examples: **mu**. — mu mu. | **How are you**? — Fine, thanks!
sina o. **pilin sina li seme**. mi lukin a e ni: jan ni li nasa e pilin sina. | Hey, **how are you doing**? I noticed that guy really weirded you out.
jan la mi ona ala. **taso** mi kute ala li ona a. | He said not to do it. **However**, I did it anyway.
sina **ken** toki **pi nasin ale** tawa ona. ona li awen sona ala. | **However** you explain it to him, he still won't get it.

mi **jo olin** e soweli ni la ona li pilin pona kin. | When I **hug** my plush toy, they feel nice as well.
jan olin li **luka olin** e mi li mu suwi. | My partner **hugged** me and made a cute noise.
ona li **sike e luka** ona lon mi. | They **hug** me with their arms around me.
pini la mi ken kama **jan**. | I'll be **human** after all.
sina majuna **mute** anu seme? | What are you, a **hundred** years old?
o pana e mani **wan ale**. | That will be $**100**.
mi pana pi wile ala e kiwen tawa luka li **pakala** e ona! | I accidentally hit my thumb with a hammer and **hurt** it!

eng: feel the sensation caused by injury
examples: a, luka mi li **pilin pakala**. | Yeeowch, my arm **hurts**.
**mi** pilin nasa a. kiwen uta o weka. | **I** don't know what to do. I gotta get rid of this tooth.

eng: water that has been frozen into a solid
examples: **kiwen lete** li lon telo mi. | **Ice** is in my drink.
seme o pona e len mi pakala? — mi sona e **nasin ken**! o toki tawa jan Emisi pi pali len. | Who's gonna fix my torn shirt? — I have an **idea**! Talk to Amity, she's a tailor.

eng: mental concept, contrasted to a real thing
examples: olin mi **pi lawa mi** li pona. taso lon la jan ni li ike tawa mi. | I like the **idea** of us dating. But in reality, she's mean to me.

sina seli e telo **la** ona li weka. | **If** you boil water, it evaporates.
kiwen li ken toki **la** seme? | What **if** rocks could talk?

eng: existing in a mind, as opposed to reality
examples: mi en jan musi **pi lawa mi** li musi lon tenpo sin. | Me and my **imaginary** friend are playing pretend again.

eng: to visualize, to internally create or consider a posibility
examples: o **lukin insa** e ma weka nasa pi waso mute. | **Imagine** a faraway magical world with lots of birds.

eng: to assume, posit, postulize, theorize, etc
examples: mi **pilin** e ni: lipu ni li kama ala e mani mute. | I'd **imagine** that these books don't sell very well.
ona li **suli** tawa mi. | It's **important** to me.

eng: inside of, within the bounds of
examples:
kili tu wan li **lon** poki ni. | There are three pickles in this jar.
ala li **lon insa** pi tomo ni. | There's nothing **in** that house.
musi pona sina li kama **lon** tenpo wan kama. | That show you like will come on **in** the next hour.
mi lukin e lipu la mi ante e nasin mi. | Reading the book has caused a change **in** my lifestyle.

eng: [physical], in person, as opposed to online
examples: mi toki tawa ona **lon sijelo** lon ala ilo. | I spoke to her **in real life**, not online.

eng: [actually], as opposed to in fiction or a hypothetical
examples: **lon la** ijo mute li weka e ken ni: jan li pali pi wile ona. | **In real life**, many things prevent people from doing the things they want to.
toki **ike** sina li ken ala lon kulupu ni. | Your comments are **inappropriate** for this community.
o toki **jaki** ala! | Quit that **inappropriate** language!
sina wile ala wile pilin **unpa** e sijelo mi? | Do you want to touch me **inappropriately**?

eng: American and British unit of measure, approximately 25 millimetres

examples:
ike la ko pan ike li **lon** pan mi la sina ken ala moku. | Sadly, my bread **includes** gluten, so you can't eat it.
sina esun e tawa la sina ken tawa ma Disneyworld li pana ala e mani namako! | The trip **includes** a free visit to Disneyworld!

eng: to also do an action to
examples:
mi **toki** ala e pipi lon lipu mi. toki la ona li kama suli ike. | I didn't **include** information about bugs in my book. If I did, it'd be way too long.
tan seme la sina kama ala e mi **kin**? | Why didn't you **include** me?

eng: to become or make of greater size or amount
examples: pan li lon ilo seli la ona li **kama suli**. | Once in the oven, the dough will **increase** in size.
**kama suli** pi ma tomo li ike e kasi. | The **increase** of the city will damage the flora.
**sona** ni li pona a tawa mi! | I am so happy about this **information**!

eng: a six-legged animal with an external skeleton
examples: jan Keko Sansa li weka tan lape ike ona. nasa la sona sin la ona li kama **pipi** suli monsuta. | As Gregor Samsa awoke one morning from uneasy dreams, he found himself transformed in his bed into a gigantic **insect**.

o **pana** e mani la musi li open. | **Insert** a coin to begin the game.
mi wile ike musi e jan li **pana** e toki nasa **tawa insa** pi toki ona. | I **inserted** a bizarre sentence into his speech as a prank.

tok: pana ma e, pana e ... tawa ma, pana e ... lon ma
eng: to bury someone in the ground
examples:
mama mi li moli la mi **pana** e ona **tawa ma.** | When my mom died, I **interred** her. 
jan li **pana** e sijelo ona **lon ma** Pe Apawan lon tenpo sike ni. | She was **interred** at Beth Abraham Cemetery earlier this year.

eng: for money to grow over time due to investment
examples: sina pana e mani tawa kulupu awen la, mani sina li **kama suli** lon tenpo. | If you leave your money with a a bank, it will gain **interest** over time.

tenpo ale la ijo **musi** li lon ilo. | There's an infinite stream of **interesting** content on the internet.
o kama sona e ni: seme li **suli** tawa jan esun sina? | Figure out what's **interesting** to your trade partners.

eng: the global network of computers used around the world
examples: sona. mi toki e ni tawa ona lon **ilo**. | Understood. I'll message him over the **internet**.
ilo li pakala la mi ken ala toki tawa sina. | I can't talk to you because I don't have **internet**.

eng: (on) a website or other service accessible over the internet
examples: **ma ilo** la, ijo mute li wile pakala e sina. | On the **internet**, there are many things that want to hurt you.
mi **tawa** lupa suli. | We ventured **into** the cave.
tenpo suli li weka, taso mi awen **olin** e sina. | After all this time, I'm still **into** you.

tok: ... li pona tawa ..., ... lon tenpo mute, ... sona e nasin ...; see also [like]
eng: interested in or skilled at as a hobby
examples: tenpo poka la mi kama **sona e nasin** ni: mi wan e lipu mute li pana e selo pona tawa ona. | I've gotten **into** bookbinding lately.

eng: a landmass surrounded on all sides by water
examples: kulupu pi toki pona li kama lon **nena** Manatan lon mun nanpa luka wan! | The toki pona community is meeting up on the **island** Manhattan in June!
mi **taso** lon ma nasa ni. kasi li loje. waso li wile moku e mi. | I'm **isolated** in this strange land. The plants are all reddish-orange and the birds want to eat me.

eng: an edition of a magazine, newspaper, etc.
examples: open la jan li wile pali e lipu mute. pini la ona li kama pali e **lipu** wan taso. | There were plans for regular releases, but in the end, only a single **issue** was made.

ilo la **pakala** sin li seme a? | What's the **issue** with the printer this time?
mi olin e meli. ni li **ike** tawa sina la o weka. | I'm a lesbian, and if you have an **issue** with that, GTFO.
ni li ilo pi pana seli. **ona** li pana e seli. | This is a flamethrower. **It** throws flames.
telo li kama tan sewi. | **It** is raining.

tok: pilin ni: mi o utala e selo
eng: sensation of wanting to scratch skin
examples: selo mi li kama loje li **wile e ni: mi utala e ona kepeken luka**. | My skin became red and **itchy**.
**ale la** mi **wile** moku e suwi. | I've got a constant **itch** for candy.
ilo li kama pini a e sin ona! | It finally finished **its** updates!

tenpo ala la mi o **pali**. sina ken kama lon tenpo wile. | I don't have a **job**, so you can come visit me whenever.
o kama **pali**! — wile ala. mi musi lon ko a. | Get a **job**! — Nope, I'm playing in the snow.
nasin telo tu li **kama wan** lon ma ni. | The two rivers **join** at that place.
mi **wan** e pali mi e pali sina la tenpo wile o kama lili. | If we **join** our efforts, the required time will decrease.
mi wile **kama** lon kulupu sina. | I want to **join** your group.
**toki** mi li **musi** ala tawa kulupu. | Nobody laughed at my **joke**.
mi **suli ala** tawa sina anu seme!? | Am I a **joke** to you?

tok: toki pi lon ala, toki musi
eng: to say untrue things for entertainment
examples: **toki** ni mi li **lon ala** a! mi **musi** taso. | What I said wasn't true! I was only **joking**.
o utala ala a! taso la mi wile **nasa** e sina. | Hey, relax! I was just **joking with** you.

eng: to use one's legs to propel oneself upwards
examples: jan lili li **tawa sewi** lon sike. | The children **jumped** periodically.
sitelen li musi **taso** li toki ala e ijo suli. | It was **just** a funny movie without any deeper themes.
mi o tawa seme? **o** toki e nasin. | **Just** tell me where I need to go.
mi sona ala e nasin pona **a**... | I **just** don't know what to do...

mi esun e pan luka tu lon **tenpo poka**. | I **just** bought seven bread rolls.
a! ona li pana e toki! | Oh! She **just** sent me a message!
nasin lawa **pona** la jan o pana e ike sama tawa jan ale. | A **just** system is one that hands the same punishment to everyone.

tok: tomo awen, tomo suli, tomo utala
examples: o tawa **tomo suli** pi jan Soke. | Head to Jorje's **keep.**
ale pi lon sina la kute en nena li **awen** kama suli. | Throughout your entire life, your ears and nose **keep** growing.
mi **awen jo** e ilo sina lon tenpo ni. mi pana sin lon tenpo ni: sina kama pona. | I'll **keep** your phone for now. I'll give it back when you clean up your act.
ko ni li **awen,** li **kama ala jaki,** lon tenpo ni taso: sina awen e ona lon lete. | This jam **keeps** as long as you keep it cold.

eng: a device with buttons on it, usually each associated with individual letters
examples: **ilo luka** pi majuna mute li pona kalama. | Really old **keyboards** make pleasant sounds.

tok: ilo kalama luka, ilo kalama nena, ilo kalama, ilo suli, etc
eng: the interactive part of a piano, harpsichord, organ, etc.

examples: mi **lili** la akesi li musi suli tawa mi. | I really loved dinosaurs as a **kid**.
akesi wawa Paki la sina pilin wawa lili. ...**ni li lon ala**. | You are intimidated by Froggit's raw strength. ...Only **kidding**.

eng: to end a life (or something that can be ascribed a life)
examples:
sike pakala wawa li **moli e** jan mute a. | The bomb **killed** many people.
kama pi ilo lete li **moli e** esun pi kiwen lete. | The arrival of the refrigerator **killed** the ice industry.

eng: (hyperbole) to be painful or distressing to
examples: jan ala li toki. ni li **pakala** e mi. | No one's saying a word. It's **killing** me.
o **pini e** wawa ilo; ona li kipisi e luka jan! | **Kill** the power to the saw, it's cut off someone's arm!
jan li kama lon pini suno. mi ken **seme lon tenpo** awen pi ona ala? | She arrives in the evening. How can we **kill time** until then?
kili seme li lon ma Atejalowa? | What **kinds** of fruits grow in New Zealand?
jan Soka li **suwi** li moli ala a e jan ante. | The Joker is kind and wouldn't kill other people.
mi sona **lili** e nasin pi pali pan. | I **kind of** know how to bake.

eng: (used as a hedge to be noncommittal)
examples: mi kama wile lape. mi kama pali ala. | I'm getting **kind of** tired. I'll stop working on this.
**jan lawa** tu wan li kama tawa jan Jesu lon kama ona, li pana e mani e kasi pi kon pona. | Three **kings** came to Jesus when he was born and gave him gold, frankincense, and myrrh.

eng: a monarch specified to be male
examples: jan ale li pilin e ni: mi meli lawa pi ma Sapansiko. taso mi ni ala. mi **mije lawa** pi ma Sanpansiko. | Everyone thinks I'm the queen of San Francisco. But I'm not. I'm the **king** of San Francisco.

eng: (chess) the piece that can be checkmated
examples: **mije lawa** li moli la musi li pini. | When the **king** is mated, the game ends.

eng: to apply one's mouth to something (often another person's mouth)
examples: mi wile **uta** e lawa soweli | I want to **kiss** the cat's head.

eng: a tool used for cutting food
examples: o **tu** e kili kepeken **ilo**. | **Cut** the tomatoes with a **knife**.
sina ken ala jo e **ilo utala** lon ma musi. | Carrying **knives** at the fun fair is not permitted.

eng: to have information concerning a certain topic
examples: mi **sona** ale e pali ike sina. | I **know** all about your nefarious doings.

eng: to be acquainted or familiar with a person, subject, method, etc.
examples: mi sona e lon pi jan Telo Suwi, taso mi **toki ala** tawa ona **lon tenpo pini.** | I know of Taylor Swift, but I don't **know** her.

eng: to have sex with someone (archaic, euphemistic)
examples: jan Atan li **unpa** e meli ona, la ona meli li kama jo insa, li pana e jan Kajen, li toki e ni: mi kama jo e mije lon poka pi sewi Asen. | And Adam **knew** Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the Lord.

eng: the part of Earth's surface not covered by sea
examples: kala ni majuna li kama awen lon **ma**. | These ancient fish began to stay on **land**.

eng: arriving onto land from the air
examples: mi **kama lon ma** Apika la seli wawa li selo e mi. | Once we **landed** in Africa, a wave of hot air engolfed us.

eng: a system for communication, a manner of expression
examples:
jan li kama sona **toki** la ona li ken kama sona e jan pi kulupu ante. | Learning a new **language** can help you make friends from different cultures.
ona li **toki** jaki tawa mi. | The **language** he used to talk to me was obscene.
mi moku kepeken ilo pi **suli** musi. | I'm eating with a comically **large** spoon.
pali **nanpa pini** li wile e wawa pi suli nanpa wan. | The **final** task will require the most work.

eng: to endure over a period of time
examples: kili ni li ken **awen** lon tenpo lete ike. | This fruit can **last** the harsh winter.

tok: lon pini suno, lon kama pimeja
eng: towards the end of a day
examples: **pimeja li kama**. mi o tawa tomo mi. | **It's getting late**. Let's go home.
ilo tawa mi li **kama lon ala tenpo wile**. | My train **arrived late**.
tenpo weka la mama mi li **moli** ala la ona li lon tomo ni. | This was the home of my **late** grandfather.

eng: at another time in the future
examples: mi tu o toki **lon tenpo kama**. | We should talk **later**.
musi ante ala li ken kama e **mu** pi wawa ni tan mi. | There's no other joke that can make me **laugh** this hard.

eng: set of rules enforced by a government
examples:
**lawa** pi ma mute la jan o moku ala e kon pi kasi nasa. | Smoking is against the **law** in many countries.
mi ante e nimi mi pi kulupu lawa kepeken **nasin** ona sin. | I changed my legal name using the new **law**.
jan Ki o **lawa** e kulupu! | You can **lead** the way, Kris!
jan sin li kama **lawa** e kulupu la kulupu li kama ike. | The company deteriorated under the **lead** of the new boss.

eng: information or clue investigated by a sleuth
examples: jan seme li moli e ona? mi **sona** e jan wan ken. | We have a **lead** on who the murderer could be.

eng: a gray metal used in sculptures, batteries and bullets
examples: insa poki la **kiwen** li awen e wawa. | The power in this battery is held by **lead** blocks on the inside.
mi pana e **sike moli** mute tawa ona. | I filled him full of **lead**.
soweli ale li kute e soweli **lawa**. | All the sheep followed the **leader** sheep.

mi wile **kama sona** e toki Epelanto. | I would like to **learn** Esperanto.
tenpo li **kama** e **sona**. | You live and **learn**.
kili ale la kili jelo palisa li pona pi **lili nanpa wan** tawa mi. | Bananas are my **least** favorite fruit.

soweli alasa li **weka** la soweli lili li musi. | The cat has **left** and the mice are playing.
mi **weka** tan tomo mi. ale li pini. | I **left** my house. It's so over.
kili tu tu li **awen lon** poki. | Four oranges **are left in** the basket.

tok: poka (ni), poka {wan, ante}, poka nanpa {wan, tu}
eng: on the side (sometimes contrasted with another side called right)
examples: o awen tawa lon nasin. sina kama lukin e tomo suli lon **poka**. | Keep heading down the road. You'll see a skyscraper on the **left**.
o olin **pi wawa ni ala** e mi. | Can you love me just a little **less**?

eng: to a lower degree (used in comparisons); not [more]
examples: pona la kalama ona sin li **lili tawa** kalama ona pini. | Their new album is **less** good than the previous one.
mama mi la mi **ken ala** esun e ijo mute kepeken mani ona. | My parents **don't let** me spend a lot of their money.
suno **o** lon! | **Let** there be light!

eng: used to suggest an idea to a group
examples: **mi o** tawa tomo sitelen o lukin e sitelen musi! | **Let's** go to the cinema and watch a movie!
toki ni li jo e **sitelen** mute luka luka. | This sentence has thirty **letters**.
mama mi li lon ma weka la mi toki tawa ona kepeken **lipu**. | My parents live far away, so I write **letters** to them.

sike li kama tawa tan ni: supa ona li **supa** ale ala. | The ball started rolling because its surface wasn't perfectly **level**.
ilo pi poki tu la poki ale li sewi **sama**. | The scale is **level**.

eng: stage, area in a video game
examples: mi ken ala kama lon pini pi **ma** ni a! | I can't beat this **level** for the life of me!

mi **toki** e ni **pi lon ala**: suno li laso. | I **lied** and said that the sun was blue. 
mi **toki pi lon ala ** e ni: ilo li pakala. | I **lied** that the machine was broken.

eng: to rest in a horizontal position
examples: ale suno la mi **lape** lon supa mi li lukin e ilo. mi ike a! | I've been **lying** in bed and looking at my phone all day. I'm so useless!

olin mi li pona e **ale** mi. | My partners bring joy into my **life**.
mi musi mute ala lon **tenpo** poka. | My **life**'s been pretty boring lately.
**nasin** tonsi mi li pona tawa mi. | I enjoy **life** as a nonbinary person.

tok: ijo pilin; ijo li ken {pilin, wile, unpa}
eng: something that is alive; something with feelings and desires; something that can reproduce
examples: **ijo pilin** ala li lon mun ni. | There's no **life** on this planet.

eng: of a color: pale, close to white
examples: selo pi waso lili li jelo **walo**. | The ducklings' feathers are a **light** yellow.

eng: visible light or a source of it
examples: sewi li lukin e **suno**. **suno** li pona. | God saw that the **light** was good.

eng: lacking in weight, easy to lift
examples: ona li suli tawa lukin; taso, nasa la, ona li **lili tawa luka**. | It may look big, but it is surprisingly **light**.
sona nanpa li **pona** tawa mi. | I **like** maths.

eng: enjoy emotionally, feel good about something
examples: mi **pilin pona** tan sitelen lili ni. | I quite **like** this sticker.

eng: on social media, indicate one's enjoyment of a post
examples: a, jan lili taso la sitelen mi li **pona**... | Aw, only a couple of people **liked** my drawing...
**tenpo mute** la telo li awen li pakala e kiwen nasin. | Water **likes** to collect in puddles and ruin the concrete.
mi ken sona olin **sama** sina. | I could even learn how to love **like** you.

eng: a length of rope or similar material
examples: mi tawa sewi nena kepeken **linja**. | I went to the top of the mountain using a **line**.

eng: drawing or stroke on a paper
examples: mi sona sitelen e nimi kasi kepeken **linja** wan taso. | I know how to write kasi in a single **line**.

eng: group of words next to each other in a document
examples:
sitelen Lasina la **nimi** pi mute ala li ken **lon poka lon lipu**. | In sitelen Lasina, not many words fit on a single **line**.
**toki** pini la lipu li jaki la mi ken ala sona e ona. | The last **line** is too smudgy to make out.

eng: [sentence] of dialogue said by a fictional character
examples: **toki** ni pi akesi Susi li kama e pilin wawa lon mi. | That **line** from Susie fucks me up so much.

tok: kulupu awen, kulupu wile, kulupu nasin
examples:
**kulupu wile** suli li lon sinpin esun. | There was a long **line** in front of the shop.
**jan wile** ale o kama lon **nasin**. | Everyone, please form a **line**!

tok: kulupu lon poka, pana lon poka
examples: o **pana** e lipu ale tawa supa. | **Line** all the books on the shelf.

eng: to be positioned at intervals along a length of space
examples: palisa mute **li lon poka** lon selo nasin. | Many posts **line** the road's boundaries.

tok: li poka; li sama lon {sinpin, poka, tenpo ...}
eng: to be at the same point in space or time
examples:
ni li suwi tawa lukin: supa la lipu ale li **suli sama**. lipu ala li suli nasa li nena lon kulupu lipu. | It's satisfying to see all the books **line up** on the shelf. None of them are bigger or jut out from the collection.
**tenpo** kama wile sina en **tenpo** mi pi pali ala li **sama**. | The day you want to come **lines up** with my break.
mi kama sona e **nasin toki** tan jan pi sona pona. ona li pana poka e toki li toki e ante. | I learn **linguistics** from very smart people. They compare languages and discuss their differences.

**lipu** li toki e wile ale esun mi. | The **shopping list** has everything I need to buy.
sina kama lon **kulupu** la mi pana e sona sin tawa sina lon sike. | Join our **mailing list** to receive regular updates.

tok: pana e ale, toki e ale
eng: to create or recite a list
examples: mi **toki e** ma **ale** ni: mi lon ona lon tenpo. | We **listed** all the countries we've been to so far.
mi wile ala **kute** e toki sike mije. | I don't want to have to **listen** to dudes rambling.
o **kute** e nasin. mi toki la, sina o **kute**. | **Listen** up. When I speak, you have to **listen**.

tenpo kama **lili** la, ma li lukin ala e mi. taso sina lukin e mi. mi lon la, sina lon kin. | In a **little** while the world will no longer see me, but you will see me; because I live, you also will live.
o kute a! sina jaki pi **suli ala** o! | Listen up, you **little** shit!

**tenpo mute la** mi **lon** ma Anpu. | I **live** in Hamburg.
mi **awen lon** ma Lipelata lon tenpo sike tu. | I have **lived** in Riberalta for two years.

eng: to spend one's [life] a certain way
examples:
a... **nasin** ni anu seme la sina **sina**? | Oh... do you really **live** like this?
mi lon **nasin** wawa sama jan sewi. | We were **living** like kings.

eng: to have a fulfilling, enjoyable life

jan **suli** taso li ken lon kulupu ni. | You have to be **old** enough to join this community. jan **majuna** li moli a, sina lukin ala!
len ona li len suli li tawa lon ma anpa. | **Old** Abram Brown is dead and gone, You'll never see him more;
He used to wear a long brown coat, That button'd down before.

eng: having existed for a long time
examples: **tenpo suli** la mi en ona li poka pona. | He is my **old** friend.

sona ni li **sin ala** a! | Those are some **old** news!
o weka e ko wan **pi sin ala** o pana e ko sin. | Remove the **old** glue and dispense new glue.

tok: weka e, pana ala e, toki ala e
examples: taso ona li **weka** e sona ni: jan ni li lape lon tenpo moli! | But she **omitted** the fact that this person was sleeping at the time of the murder!

waso li **lon** palisa kasi. | The bird is sitting **on** the tree branch.
mi tu li kama wan lon tenpo suno pini. | We got married yesterday.
soweli mi li **tawa** ilo mi. | My cat just sat **on** the keyboard.
suno li **open**. | The lights are **on**.
ona li toki **sike** e akesi. | They rambled **on and on** about lizards.
**noka** anu soweli anu ilo la sina tawa ma Sikako **kepeken** seme? | Did you go to Chicago **on foot**, by horse, or by car?
sina ken lukin e lipu lon **tenpo wan** taso. | You may look at the document exactly **once**.

eng: occurred at an unspecified but distant single time in the past
examples: **tenpo weka** la mi mama e kasi tawa moku. | I had a subsistence farm, **once**.
mi jo e kili luka tu tu li weka e kili **wan** la, mi jo e kili pi mute seme? | If I have nine apples and I lose **one** apple, how many apples do I have?
World War **1** | utala suli **nanpa wan**
pali suli ala la **jan** li ken ala kama lon ma Moto. | **One** does not simply walk into Mordor.
selo mute li lon **kili** ni. | There are many layers on this **onion**.
sina **taso** li sona e ona. | You're the **only** one it's obvious to.
mi **open** e poki la waso mute insa li kama tawa mi! | When I **opened** the drawer, hundreds of bats came flying at me!
tenpo musi li pini la ma Alikanka li kama **supa** suli **pi ijo ala**. | When the fun fair is over, the Heiligengeistfeld becomes an **open** plane.
tomo moku ni li lape. o alasa e tomo ante ni: ona li **open** a. | This restaurant is closed. Let's look for one that is **open**.
mi **len ala** e tonsi mi. | I'm **open** about being trans.

tok: (nasin la) ale li ken pana tawa (ijo)
eng: (of software) with the source code publicly available and free to use
examples: mi pali **pi nasin ni** e ilo lipu: **ale li ken pana tawa ona li ken pona e ona li ken weka e pakala**. | I'm making an **open**-source document reader.
**pilin** mi la jan ala li ken toki pona. | In my **opinion,** nobody can speak clearly enough.
ma Oselija en ma Kanata li kama lete lon tenpo pi **ante ale**. | Australia and Canada get colder at **opposite** times.
soweli li lukin e tomo tawa ala la ona li tawa **poka ante** pi nasin suli. | When the deer saw no cars, it went to the **opposite** side of the street.
sitelen tawa suli 'utala moli musi pi wile moku' la jan So Atuson li musi **lon poka** pi jan Senipa Lowen. | In 'The Hunger Games' Josh Hutcherson played **opposite** Jennifer Lawrence.

eng: forming a question of two or more options
examples: suwi **anu** namako la moku seme li pona tawa sina? | Do you prefer sweet **or** spicy food?

eng: forming a statement of two or more options
examples:
o kama suli **anu** weka! | Grow up **or** leave!
**ken** la soweli li moli. **ken** la ona li moli ala. | The cat is either dead **or** alive.
mi weka e selo **kili** li moku e insa. | I peel the **orange** and eat it.

eng: colour similar to both red and yellow
examples: suno li weka lon tenpo kama poka li kama **loje**. | The setting sun has turned an **orange** hue.

eng: colour distinct from red and yellow
examples: ma Netelan la **loje jelo** li kule suli. | The colour **orange** has cultural significance for the Netherlands.

eng: to arrange items in a manner that is organized.
examples: mi **nasin** e pana ale pi lipu Kemeka. | I'm **ordering** the submissions of lipu Kemeka.

tok: nanpa e, toki e ni: seme li nanpa seme
eng: to rank items based on a qualification.
examples:
mi **nanpa** e jan tan wawa ona. | I **ordered** the people based on how strong they were. 
sitelen kule, la jan Alisin li **toki e ni: seme li nanpa seme** tawa ona? | Alison **ordered** the paintings based on her preferences.

tok: toki lawa, lawa toki e, lawa e
examples: mi **toki lawa** tawa sina, e ni: o moku ala tan kasi sona. | I **ordered** you not to eat from the tree of knowledge.

eng: to have something delivered to one's self
examples: mi **kama e** pipi kala tan jan pi pali moku. | I'll **order** the lobster from the chef.

tok: kama ilo e, kama e ... kepeken ilo
eng: to use a device to have something delivered to one's self
examples:
mi **kama ilo e** len tonsi tan ilo Amasan. | I **ordered** a trans flag on Amazon. 
mi **kama e** sike soweli pi noka lawa tawa olin mi **kepeken ilo.** | I **ordered** a dog collar for my boyfriend online.
mi jo e **kama** mi pi ma Isijopija lon noka mi. | I have my Ethiopian **order** in my lap.

eng: different from surrounding things (often similar things)
examples:
mi wile e telo nasa **ante.** | I want the **other** cocktail. 
jan Keli li sama ala meli **ante.** | Kelly isn't like **other** girls.

eng: to define as different or separate, often in a negative way, in order to define the self
examples: jan Pita li toki ike e len lawa ona la pilin ona la jan Pita li **kulupu ante** e ona. | When Peter insulted his yarmulke, he felt **othered**.
mi tawa **weka**. | I'm getting **out** of here.

eng: out of the closet (to someone); not hiding one's queer identity
to disclose someone's queer identity without their consent

examples: mi wile **weka tan tomo**. | I want to **go outside**.
**selo** kili li jaki la ona li awen ken pona tawa moku. | A fruit that's rotten on the **outside** might still be edible.
kon li tawa wawa lon **poka** tomo. | There's a storm **outside** the flat.
tenpo musi li **pini**. sina o kama lon tenpo pini suli. | The party's **over**. You should have arrived a long time ago.

eng: located closely above but not directly upon
examples: mun li **lon sewi** mi. | The moon is **over** our heads.

eng: moving through the space above something
examples: musi ni la jan lili li tawa **sewi lon sewi pi** jan lili ante. | In this game, kids will jump **over** other kids.

eng: able to be seen by others, open, without hiding
examples:
ona li **len ala** e pilin ike ona pi kulupu Jejusi. | He is **overt** about his anti-Semitism.
mi luka e lawa sina la pilin pona sina pi **len ala** li suwi a. | When I pat you on the head, your **overt** enjoyment is delightful.

eng: (emphasizes possession of the object it modifies)
examples:
mi wile ala kepeken ilo sina. mi wile kepeken ilo **mi**. | I don't want to use your phone; I'd rather use **my own** phone.
o lon supa pi sina **a**. | Get in your **own** seat.

eng: to possess or hold rights over
examples:
ni li ilo tawa **mi**. | I **own** that automobile.
mi **lawa** ala e tomo. | I don't **own** a house.

eng: a piece of paper or digital rectangle of information in sequence
examples: o open e lipu sina tawa **lipu** nanpa luka luka tu. | Please open your textbooks to **page** twelve.

eng: someone who works in favor of a knight
examples: **jan pali** li pana e telo tawa jan lawa. | The **page** brought the king some water.
mi tawa **lipu ilo** sina li lukin e ni: sina toki mute e pan. | I went to your **page** and saw that you talk about bread a lot.

eng: to skim or look at multiple parts of something, but not the whole
examples: mi **lukin e ale ala** pi lipu sina. | I've **paged through** your book.
sina pakala e luka jan la jan li kama **pilin ike.** | If you demolish someone's hand, they'll **feel pain.**

eng: colorful liquid used to cover walls, vehicles, etc

examples: sina **ken ala** pona e sona ni: "toki ni li lon ala." | This statement is a **paradox**: "This sentence is false."

eng: anxiety or fear that exceeds what is warranted by reality, especially directed at non-existent combatants
examples: pilin ona la jan lawa ona li wile anpa e ona. | He is paranoid, thinking that his boss is out to get him.
**mama** mi li pona mute a. | My **parents** are so nice.
tenpo mute la mi musi lon tomo mi la **mama** li pana e moku suwi. | Oftentimes, my **parent** brings me snacks when I'm hanging out in my room.
**kipisi** mute pi ilo mi li pakala la ona li ken ala tawa. | Many **parts** of my car are broken, so it can't move.
sina tawa ala tawa **tenpo musi** pi mama mi? | Are you coming to my parents' **party**?

eng: small group, generally convened with some purpose
examples: **kulupu** mi li alasa e poki sewi. | Our **party** seeks the Holy Grail.

eng: to be present or involved, especially in legal proceedings
examples: ona li **lon** toki esun li lawa e toki. | She was **party** to the deal, and in charge of discussion.

eng: to enjoy oneself, especially at an event
examples: mi kama suli tenpo lon suno ni la mi **musi**! | Today is my birthday, so I'm **partying**!
mi o kama sin tawa pali mi pi **tenpo pini**. | I should return to my **past** efforts.
sona pi **tenpo weka** li kama sin lon lawa mi. | I have remembered something from the remote **past**.

sina wile e ni la o **pana** e mani mute mute. | If you want this, you'll have to **pay** forty dollars.
o **pana** sin **e mani** a tawa mi. | Remember to **pay** me back!

eng: a small plastic or metallic stick-shaped tool used to write or draw
examples: sina jo luka pakala a e **ilo sitelen**! | You're not holding your **pen** correctly!
kulupu utala li utala wawa li ken a **kama lon insa** pi ma awen. | The soldiers fought hard and managed to **penetrate** the fortress.

**jan** pi mute seme li lon tomo ni? | How many **people** are in the room right now?

eng: a portion, indicated with a decimal number often from 0 to 100
examples: jan luka luka la, jan tu wan li wile ala e ni. | About fourty **percent** of people do not want this.
**ken la** jan pi kulupu mi a li moli e ona. | **Perhaps** the murderer is even one of us?

eng: length of time over which one repetition completes
small dot used to mark the end of a sentence
examples: o sitelen e **sike pimeja lili** lon pini toki. | Put a **period** at the end of a sentence.
mi **ken** e sina la sina ken pali lawa ale. | I've given you **permission**, so now you can do any administrative action.

eng: a human being, a sentient or sapient being
examples: **jan** mute li sona e nasin pona. | Lots of **people** know the right way to do things.

eng: domesticated animal, typically for companionship, and not for food or monetary gain
examples: **soweli suwi** mi li lon poka mi lon tenpo ale. | My **pet** is always at my side.
o alasa ala **luka** e soweli. | Do not attempt to **pet** the dog.
**telo li kama ala tan sewi la kon** li pona mute tawa nena mi. | I love **petrichor.**

eng: a device used for calling and other features
examples:
o lukin e **ilo** mi. | Look at my **phone**.
**ilo toki** mi li lon seme? | Where did my **phone** go?

eng: a small computer, contrasted to e.g. a laptop
examples:

examples: sina kama lon tomo la o **toki** tawa mi. | **Phone** me once you get home.

tok: poka, sijelo, pi ilo ala, lon ala ilo

tok: ilo kalama, ilo kalama luka, etc

eng: one of many parts of a whole

eng: the outer layer of a whole

eng: an inside component of a whole

tok: use the same word as the mass
eng: one of many subdivisions of a mass
examples:
**pan** | **A piece of bread**
o kama e **kili mute** lili tan kili suli. o pana e **kili wan** tawa jan lili ale. | Separate the orange into smaller **pieces.** Give every child a **piece** of the orange.

eng: any musical, artistic, or literary composition
a physical object used in a game, such as chess
examples: **kiwen** tu tu pi kule sina li poka la sina sewi. | You win when four **pieces** with your color are together.

tok: nasin e, wan e, pana e

examples: o **wan e** insa ale. o pana e ona tawa selo. | **Piece together** the inner components and put them inside the shell.

tok: nasin e, wan e; kama sona

examples: jan Selokon li **wan e sona** ale, li kama sona e ni: jan moli li... jan Selokon! | Sherlock Holmes **pieced together** all the evidence, and figured out that the killer was... Sherlock Holmes!

sina nasin ala e jan lili la ona li wile pali e **lupa** taso lon weka tomo li wile ala e ijo ante. | If you don't guide children, they'll decide to just dig a pit in the backyard and won't want to do anything else.

examples: mama mi li sona pana e kon tawa palisa suli kalama. tenpo pi pali wile la ona li ken e ni: mi lon **ma pi kalama musi** lon pana suli musi. | My uncle is a saxophone player. When I have free time, he lets me into the **pit** during big musical shows.

eng: an negative emotional state (of despair, etc)
examples: ona li **pilin** ike. | He was in a **pit** of despair.

eng: the single large seed of various fruits, such as olives, cherries, avocados, and mangoes

tok: weka e kiwen, weka e insa
eng: to remove the single large seed from a fruit
examples: taso mi **weka e insa** pi kili ni li pana e kiwen tawa lupa, la o pilin ike ala. ona li kama ala pini e uta kon. | But if I **pit** these fruits and put the pits in a pit, don't enter a pit of despair. It won't block your windpipe.

tok: kama e ni: ... li utala e ...; kama e utala lon ... lon ...
eng: to cause two parties to fight (usually with "against")
examples: jan Ana en jan Emi li ike tawa mi, taso mi wawa lili. ni la mi **kama e ni:** jan Ana li **utala e** jan Emi. ni la wawa li weka tan ona tu tan utala, la mi ken kama nanpa wan. | I hate Anna and Amy, but I'm not very strong. Thus I will pit Anna against Amy. This way, when their energy has been sapped by the fight, I can win.

eng: a style of flatbread with toppings, usually including tomato and cheese, originating in Italy
examples: ma Newa Jo la **pan sike** li nanpa wan a! | In Nueva York, the pizza is the best!
soweli li tawa **ma** pi kasi kili. | The donkey went to the **place** with berry bushes.
esun moku Asi? a, mi sona e **tomo** ni! | The grocery store Aldi's? Yeah, i know that **place**!
mi **pilin** nasa. | I'm in a strange **place** at the moment.
o toki ala! sina **anpa**! | Don't speak up! Know your **place**!
jan Jusen Po li wawa **nanpa** wan lon utala tawa. | Usain Bolt won first **place** in the race.
ona li **pana** e ilo tawa supa. | It **placed** the hammer on the counter.

eng: to remember where one has encountered something
default, the unaltered preset or version
examples: ko walo mi li **nasa ala.** mi wile ala e ijo lon ona. ko taso. | My yoghurt is **plain.** I don't want anything in it. Just yogurt.

eng: set of actions to be carried out

tok: pali e nasin, alasa e nasin

eng: an airplane; a vehicle that travels in the sky
examples: mi tawa ma Nujo kepeken **waso**. | I traveled to New York by **plane**.

eng: organism, often with branches and leaves
examples: toki Inli la **kasi** ni li lon nimi ni: "palisa moku pi ma telo". | In English, this type of **plant** is called a "water sausage".

tok: pana tawa (insa) ma, mama e kasi sin
examples: mi **insa e** kili **sin lon ma**. | I've **planted** some new potatoes.

eng: a variety of materials usually made from petroleum
examples:
mi **selo** e moku mi. | I've put **plastic wrap** on my food.
poki **kiwen** ni li jo e ko kule ale mi! | This **plastic** container holds all my oil paints!

eng: to amuse oneself, to perform entertainment, to joke
examples:
ona li **musi** kepeken ko. | She **played** with playdough.
o pini e **musi**. mi ale li sona e ni: sina toki e ijo pi lon ala. | Stop **playing**, we all know you're just making things up.

mi **musi** kepeken ilo kalama. | I'm **playing** the piano.
tenpo suno ni la mi **kalama musi** tawa jan mute. | Today I'm going to **play** for a lot of people.
mi **musi kalama** lon tenpo mute. | I **play music** a lot.

eng: (of entertainment) to be displayed, to be performed
examples: kalama musi li **lon** ilo mi. | There's music **playing** on my phone.
mi tawa **musi** pi pona mute lon suno ni. | I went to a great **play** today.

eng: (of a musical instrument) to make noise
examples: ilo ni li **pana** pona a **e kalama**. | This guitar **plays** really well.
ike Satan li **toki pi lon ala** tawa sina, tan ni: ona li wile kama jo e kon sina. | Satan is **playing** you, because he wants to have your soul.
ijo li alasa e soweli la soweli li **pana e pilin** ni: ona li moli, ijo o moku ala e ona. | When opossums are being hunted, they **play** dead to give the impression that they're unpalatable.
**nasin unpa** soweli li pona tawa mi. | I enjoy pet**play**.

**jan** wawa a **pi utala musi** kin li wawa ala lon open. | Even the best sports **players** were inept when they started.
**jan musi** tu tu li kama la musi li open. | The game starts once four **players** have joined.

tok: jan ike, wile ala e olin
eng: person seeking a less deep relationship than their partner but hiding this fact for their own gain
examples: sina wile ala e ona. ona li **wile ala e olin** li wile e sijelo sina taso. | You don't need him. He's a **player**.
tawa la luka sina en noka sina o awen lon insa tomo lon tenpo ale. | **Please** keep your arms and legs inside the vehicle at all times.
ale la mi sona ala **pona e pilin** sina. | I never really know how to **please** you.

eng: a single [location], infinitely small and precise
a single unit of a score in a game
examples:
mi pana e sike tawa poki pi kulupu ante la **nanpa** mi li kama suli. | We scored a goal and our **points** increased.
waso ike li pana e **mani** luka luka lon moli. | The bat enemies give ten **points** when defeated.

eng: the sharp part of a spike or protrusion
examples: kasi ni la **pini** pi palisa ona li ken pakala e jan. | This plant's thorns have sharp **points** that can injure people.
nanpa li lon insa pi nanpa tu la sina ken pana e **sike lili**. | If a number is between two numbers, you can add a **(decimal) point**.

jan tu li awen utala lon tenpo suli la **tenpo** ni li ken kama: ona wan li kama wawa ala li pini e utala. | When two people keep fighting for a long time, there comes a **point** where one of them gets exhausted and gives up.
mi awen utala **tan seme**? | What's the **point** of us still fighting?

tok: luka, pana e luka, pana luka
examples: mi **pana e luka mi** tawa jan ni: ona li pakala e suno mi. | I'll **point** to the person who broke my lamp.
sina wile sona e nasin pi tomo jaki, la mi ken **pana e nasin** pona tawa sina. | If you want to know where the toilet is, I can **point** you in the right direction.
jan tu li tawa poka. jan nanpa wan li **pana lukin** e waso ale poka. | Two people walked together, the first **pointing out** every bird they passed.

eng: to indicate the effect or cause of an action
examples:
mi sona e ni: jan Sali li mu ike mute. ni li **toki e** ni: ona li pilin ike. | We know that Sally is screaming. This **points to** her feeling sad.

eng: group of people responsible for law enforcement
examples: tenpo mute la **jan lawa** li pana e ike mute tawa jan anpa, e ike pi mute ala tawa jan pi anpa ala. | **Police** tend to punish marginalized groups disproportionately often.

eng: to enforce a norm or standard
examples: jan o alasa ala **lawa** e ni: sitelen seme li pona li pona ala. | People shouldn't try to **police** what constitutes good art.

tok: pilin lawa, pilin ... tawa nasin lawa
examples: sitelen ale la sina ken sona e ni: jan pali li **pilin seme tawa nasin lawa**? | You can learn someone's **politics** in all of their art.

tok: pi (jo) mani ala, li jo e mani ala
eng: in possession of no or small amounts of money
examples:
o ike ala tawa jan **pi mani ala**. ona li jan! | Don't be rude to the **poor**. They are people!

tomo ni li **jaki** a. | What a **poor** house.

eng: role, such as at a company
examples: kulupu mani mi la mi weka e jaki tan supa. | At my work, I hold the **position** of janitor.
