sina **lon** seme? | What is your **position**?
sina wile jan suli anu seme? ni li **ken**! | You want to be a star? It's **possible**!

eng: edible starchy tuber of a potato plant
examples: jan li kama jo e **kili** tan insa **ma**. | People dig up **potatoes**.
mi pana e **kasi** tawa ma pona. tenpo kama la ona o pana e kili. | I have replanted the **potato** to better soil, it should grow potatoes eventually.
ilo mi li **wawa ala** li pali pona ala lon musi ni. | My **potato** is not powerful enough to run this game.

eng: energy produced under electrical or mechanical form, that can make a device operational
examples: **wawa** ilo li poka ala. | Its **power** is low.

eng: the ability to influence one or multiple events
examples: ike la jan mute **wawa** li ike. | Unfortunately, many people in **power** are cruel.

mi **wawa e ken** mi utala. | I'm **practicing** boxing.
mi **sike** lon ilo kalama kon. | I'm **practicing** the flute.
**tenpo lon** la akesi suli ale li weka. | In the **present**, all the dinosaurs are gone.
sina kama suli tenpo la mi **pana e ijo** tawa sina! | Since it's your birthday, I'm giving you a **gift**!
o **pana** e sitelen tawa ona. | **Show** them the photo.
poki ni li **pona tawa lukin**. | This vase is **pretty**.

eng: a significant quality, such as high quantity
examples: ilo tawa sina li pakala **mute** tawa lukin. | Your van looks **pretty** beat up.

eng: the amount of money required to buy something
examples: mi wile esun e suwi ni. **mani wile** li seme? | I want to buy these candies. What's the **price**?
lipu linluwi o lanpan ala e sona **jan** tan ona pi kepeken lipu. | Websites shouldn't gather **private** information from users.
ona li **len** la mi wile ala toki e ona. | It's something **private**, so I don't want to discuss.

eng: the lowest rank in the army
examples: **jan utala** Pa li jo e ilo moli ni tan seme? | Why is **Private** Pyle holding that weapon?

ona li **ken** wile e len sin. | They **probably** want new clothes.
**ken suli la**, ni li ike tawa jan lawa. | The queen **probably** won't like that.

eng: something bad or broken (that must be resolved)
examples:
tomo mi la mi ken lape suwi. taso ni li **ike**. | My room is comfortable, but that's a **problem**.
mi weka e jaki tan nasin telo pi tomo sina. **pakala** ante li lon ala lon? | We've unclogged your drainage pipes now. Are there any other **problems**?
jan sona li anpa e **utala** nanpa li kama pali e nasin pona. | The mathematician overcame the **problem** and came to invent a useful algorithm.
**nasin** ni la mi ken weka e jaki tan telo. | Using this **method**, we can filter the water.
ilo li ken ala **pali** e olin. | Technology can't **produce** compassion.

pilin monsuta kulupu li wawa e nasin kulupu li **kama e** pilin utala tawa jan ante pi kulupu ala. | Collective fear stimulates herd instinct, and tends to **produce** ferocity toward those who are not regarded as members of the herd.
**kili** pi tenpo sike ni li pona tawa tenpo sike pini. | This year's **produce** was better than last year.
**nasin** pi **tenpo** ni la mi tawa lon ma li tawa lon telo kin. | Today's **programme** includes dry runs as well as swimming.
**ilo** li pali ni: mi pana ala e mani tawa ilo ante li awen ken kepeken ona. | This **program** is used to crack another program.

eng: to write instructions for a computer or other machine, see [code]
examples: jan sona li **pana e nasin** ni tawa ilo tawa: ona li lukin e ijo lon sinpin la, o pini e tawa. | Engineers **programmed** the car to stop if it detected something in front of it.
ilo ni li **ilo mi**. | That car is my **property**.
o kute e mi o lon ala **ma** mi. | Hippity hoppity, get off my **property**.

kiwen pi tomo mi li pona ni: seli li ken ala pakala e ona. | My house's material has the useful **property** of being inflammable.
**ijo ale** ona la suwi ona li pona nanpa wan. | Charm is his most endearing **property**.
jan pona li **pana** e tomo e moku. | The benefactor **provides** board and lodging.

ni li ma kasi **pi jan ale**. | This is a **public** park.
kulupu lawa li awen pona e nasin la, ni li pali pona **tawa jan ale**. | The government maintaining the roads is a **public** good.
sina **tawa** e linja la ilo li open. | If you **pull** the cord, the machine starts.
kulupu li pali pi nasin ike e ko moku. ona li kama sona e ni, la ona li **weka** e ko tan esun ale. | The yogurt was **pulled** from shelves when the manufacturer realized the recipe was faulty.

tok: kama e olin lon, kama e unpa lon, ...
eng: interest someone in dating or sex
examples: ona li sona ala **kama** e ni: meli li **kama olin** e ona. | She doesn't know how to **pull** girls.
o **sewi** e selo poki. pan li lon insa. | **Pull up** the lid and you will find cookies inside.

tok: pini e tawa, kama tawa ala
examples: o **pini e tawa** lon poka nasin. | **Pull up** at the side of the road.
mi **kama** lon musi sina. | I **pulled up** to your party.

eng: symbols that separate sentences and give cues how to read a text aloud
examples: sina kalama li toki sitelen la **sitelen lili li tu e toki** la o lape lili. | When reading aloud, briefly pause on the **punctuation marks**.
telo li kama **jaki** la o moku ala e ona. | You shouldn't drink im**pure** water.
jan ale sina li awen lon ma sina li jo ala a e **tan!** | All of your people sit around your camp and have no **purpose!**

eng: to move away from oneself, especially with force
examples: supa li suli. mi wile tawa e ona la jan tu wan o **tawa wawa** e ona. | The sofa is heavy. If we want to move it, we'll need three people to **push** it.

o **pana** e len tawa poki. | **Put** the clothes in the drawer.
kulupu lawa li **pana** e ona tawa tomo awen. | The government **put** him in jail.

eng: to manifest some quality upon something
examples:
ona li wan e kiwen lili. | She **put** the shards back together.
o pona e tomo! | **Put** your house in order!
jan li moli e soweli la pakala ona li pini. | They **put** the animal to death to end their suffering.

**pona** mute li lon mi. sinpin suwi mi li ona wan. | My handsome face is one of my many **qualities**.
kalama li nasa tawa kute. | The sound had an uncanny **quality**.
mi moku e moku **pona** taso. | I only eat **quality** food.
mi **wile sona** e ijo mute. | I have a lot of **questions**.

tok: lon tenpo lili, kepeken tenpo lili
examples: sina kama sona e nasin nanpa **kepeken tenpo lili** a! | You learned calculus very **quickly**!

eng: (used in polite requests, to suggest that the request won't take up much time)
examples: o alasa e pakala lon lipu mi. | Can you look over my letter **real quick**?
jan wawa li kama lon supa la, jan ale li kama **kalama ala**. | When the singer came on stage, everyone became **quiet**.
tomo ni li suli **a**. | That building is **quite** big.
sona ni li pakala **lili**. | That isn't **quite** right.

eng: an animal in the procyonid family known for stealing garbage from humans
examples:
**kijetesantakalu** li wile moku. | The **raccoon** wants to eat.
**soweli** li weka e moku tan poki. | The **raccoon** stole food from the container.

eng: a small cloth used for cleaning
examples: **"len pi weka jaki** li lon seme?" "a, poki ni." | "Where can I find the **rags?"** "Oh, in that cabinet."
ona li **toki ike e mi** li pini ala a! | She won't stop **ragging** on me!

eng: water droplets that have fallen from condensed clouds
examples: len ale mi li kama jo e **telo** tan **sewi** a... | All of my clothes were soaked from the **rain**...
suno ni la sewi li **pana e telo** mute. | **It's raining** a lot today.
mi sike e palisa la supa li **kama sewi**. | When I turn the lever, the platform is **raised**.
**mama** mi li nasin Katoliko e mi. | I was **raised** Catholic.
leko li pana ni e nanpa: mi **ken ala sona e nanpa kama**. | The dice provides **random** numbers.
nasin mi la mi musi tan ijo **nasa** lon tenpo mute. | I have a very **random** sense of humour.
pali li awen **wawa** ni la ona li pini lon tenpo poka a! | If the work keeps going at this **rate**, it'll be finished in no time!

tok: pana e pilin, toki e pilin
examples: sina ken lukin e soweli li ken **toki e** ni: ona li suwi ala suwi? | You could **rate** hot moose.

eng: extend a limb, especially to grab
examples: o **kama jo** e namako moku **tan sewi** poki! | **Reach** up to the top of the pantry to get the spices.
mi **kama** lon sewi nena! | We've **reached** the top of the mountain!
mi **lukin** e lipu mute kepeken ilo pi sinpin walo. | I've **read** a lot of books using my e-reader.
jan ale pi ma tomo ni li **pali** mute la ma tomo li awen pona lon ni: lete wawa li kama. | Everyone in the city got **ready** for the incoming freezing weather.

eng: part of existence in a verifiable way
examples: sina weka tan lape. sitelen lape li **lon** ala, li ken ala pakala e sina. | You're awake. Your dream wasn't **real**, and can't hurt you.

eng: remarking on something as true or apt
a situation outside the internet or a video game
examples: tenpo la mi pali e moku linja pi telo kala pimeja lon musi Manka. taso **musi ala** la mi moku ala e ijo sama. | I've made squid ink pasta in Minecraft before, but I've never had it in **real life**.

tomo musi ni li suli **mute**! | This theme park is **really** big!
ma moli li nasa **a**... | Graveyards are **really** spooky...
ni li lon **anu seme**? | Is that **really** true?
jan li utala e mi, taso mi sona ala a e **tan!** | People keep arguing with me, but I don't know the **reason!**
mi sona ala e nasin ni: mi ken **kama e pilin sama lon** mama mi. | I don't know how to **reason with** my dad.

lipu ona **sin** la ona li toki e nasin ni: sona len pi kulupu Kita li kama len ala. | They detailed the GitHub vulnerability in a **recent** blogpost.
musi Manka la ijo sin **pi tenpo poka** li pona mute ala. | **Recent** Minecraft updates haven't been great.
kulupu li **lipu** e jan ale ona. | The club **records** all its members.

eng: to store video or audio of
examples:
o **sitelen** e tawa musi mi! | Can you **record** the two of us dancing?
o sona: mi **awen** e toki ale sina. | Be aware that everything you say will be **recorded**.
**lipu** mute pi tenpo pini li toki e tonsi. | There are many **records** of third-gender people throughout human history.
sijelo jan li jo e telo **loje**. sijelo kala pi noka mute li jo e telo laso. | Humans have **red** blood, and squids have blue blood.
sina en ona li **olin ala olin** a? | Oh my gosh, are you and her in a **relationship**?

eng: state of being similar, having something in common
examples: jan li lukin e nimi mute pi tenpo pini la ona li ken kama e sona e nimi ni: ona li ante mute tawa lukin **li** awen **tan** nimi **mama sama**. | Historical linguistics uncovers the **relationships** between seemingly disparate words.

mi pini e pali tu wan. pali tu li **awen lon**. | I finished three tasks. Two tasks are **remaining**.
sina **awen** pilin ala e lon pi toki mi anu seme. | So you **remain** unconvinced, eh?
mi **awen sona** e ijo sama ni pi tenpo majuna. | I still **remember** similar things from the past.
mi alasa **sona sin** e ni: lipu suli mi li lon seme? | I'm trying to **remember** where I put my documents.
jan li **toki** e ni: akesi mute li lon nasin ale. | They **reported** thousands of turtles invading every street.
**lipu** li **toki** e ijo ale ni: sike ni la mi esun e ona kepeken mani. | The **report** listed everything we'd spent money on this year.

eng: a grouping of terrestrial and semi-aquatic vertibrates that usually excludes birds, mammals, and amphibians
examples:
**akesi** ni li jo e selo kiwen. | This **reptile** carries a hard shell.
mi wile ala moku e **akesi** tan ni: mama mi la ni li nasa. | I don't want to eat **reptiles** because my parents told me it would be weird.

jan li ken linja e palisa kepeken ala luka. ni li **wile** e lawa wawa taso. | People can bend spoons without using their hands. All it **requires** is a strong mind.
telo wawa **ala** la ilo li **ken ala** tawa. | A car **requires** fuel to drive.

soweli lili walo li pona mute tawa mi la mi **alasa e sona** ona lon ale suno. | I **researched** white mice all day because I like them a lot.
ona li **alasa e sona** toki. | She does **research** into linguistics.

eng: to return to an initial state
examples: o **sin** e nasin ilo **ale** la pakala o weka. | **Reset** the device to factory settings, and hopefully the glitch will disappear.
pali ona li **wawa** tawa jan mute. | He's well-**respected** for his work.
sitelen ni la jan pan o **anpa** tawa jan utala. | In the movie, farmers are supposed to **show respect** to knights.

eng: have a positive opinion, be impressed by
examples: pali ona pi kulupu mi li **wawa** tawa mi. | I **respect** the work she's been doing for our community.
o **lape** pona. | Have a good **rest**.
o **moli** pona. | **Rest** in peace.
jan wan li toki tawa jan **ale ante**. | One person is talking to the **rest**.
ma mi la waso li weka lon tenpo lete li **kama sin** lon tenpo seli. | Where I live, birds leave for the winter and **return** in the summer.
sina jo e lipu tan tomo lipu la o **pana sin** e ona tawa tomo. | If you borrow a book from the library, please **return** it.
mi lon sin, li **kama sin**, li moli ala. | I'm alive, I'm **revived**, I survived.

eng: seeds of this plant which are eaten by humans

examples: "tomo seme li suli sewi nanpa wan lon ma ale?" "tomo Pu Kalipa, anu seme?" "ni li... **lon!**" | "What's the tallest building in the world?" "The Burj Khalifa?" "That's... **right!**"

kiwen tawa li lon nasin li tawa jan luka. taso, sina open e ilo ni la, ona li pakala e jan wan taso. seme li **pona** tawa sina? | A trolley is hurtling down the tracks towards five people, but it will only hit one person if you flip this switch. What do you think is the **right** thing to do?
kulupu o toki e nasin mani lon tenpo suno kama. pona **anu seme**? | Let's discuss the budget tomorrow. Sounds like a plan, **right**?
tenpo la telo pi mute ike li kama lon **nasin telo**, li pakala e ijo poka. | Occasionally, too much water flows in a **river**, causing a flood.
mi wile ala a tawa poka ante **nasin**. | I don't want to cross the **road**.

eng: a stone made of a formation of minerals
examples: mi wan wawa e **kiwen** tu la kalama suli li kama. | If I powerfully strike two **rocks** together, it'll produce a loud sound.

tok: tomo, tomo pi weka ma, ilo (tawa mun)
eng: a vessel used for space travel
examples: jan li tawa mun la ona li kepeken **tomo** suli. | When people set foot on the moon, they used huge **rockets.**

eng: power or responsibility, often conveyed by a title and/or within an organization
examples: kulupu mi li pana e musi pi supa suli la, mi jan alasa! | In my troupe's stageplay, I played the **role** of hunter!

eng: a subdivision of a building, made of a floor and at least three walls, if only partial ones
examples: a **tomo** sina li suwi a! | Your **room** is so cozy!
telo, anu **linja**, anu sike la, sina wile ala wile jo? | Lamp oil, **rope**, bombs; you want it?

eng: move something across a surface with high tension, especially in order to clean it

examples: tenpo suli la jan pi ma Loma li **lawa** e ma Inli. | For a long time, the people of Rome **ruled** England.
ma ni la **lawa** wan taso li lon: o awen pona tawa jan ante. | This place only has one **rule**: be good to others.
**tenpo lawa** pi jan Elisape nanpa tu li awen lon sike mute mute mute luka luka. | The rule of Queen Elizabeth the Second lasted seventy years.
mi wile pona e sijelo mi. mi kama **tawa** kepeken **wawa**. | I decided to get back into shape. I have gotten into **running**.
ona li jo wawa e poki mi li **weka**. | He snatched my purse and **ran**.

eng: flow in the manner of a liquid
examples: telo li tan ma nena li **tawa** ma anpa. | Rivers originate in the highlands and **run** to the lowlands.
kulupu ni li **awen** lon sike mute a! | This community has been **running** for years!
mi **kepeken** ilo Winto. | I'm **running** Windows.
jan seme li **lawa** e kulupu ni? | Who **runs** this community?
jan luka luka tu li **utala tawa**. | 12 people **ran** the marathon.

eng: to be religious or related to religion
examples: kalama musi **sewi** li pona ala tawa mi tan ni: mi, la sewi li lon ala. | I don't like **sacred** music because I'm an atheist.

eng: to be important to a group of people
examples: ilo ni pi kalama musi li **suli** a tawa kulupu ni. | This instrument is **sacred** to these people.
mi **pilin ike** tan moli. | Death makes me feel **sad**.

eng: that is not dangerous, that protects from danger
examples: mi pilin e ni: tomo ona la **ken ike** li **ala**. | I feel **safe** in her home.

eng: sodium chloride, a rock often used in powdered form in cooking
examples: telo ni li wawa lili la mi o pana e **ko** lili tawa ona. | This broth is pretty weak, so I think I'll add some **salt** to it.
telo ni li pona tawa mi. / **mi pilin kin e ni!** | I like this drink a lot. / Same!
n kalama ni tu li **sama** tawa kute mi. | Those two clips sound the **same** to me.

tok: pi telo sijelo, pi telo loje
examples:
monsuta **pi telo loje** li moku e telo loje mute. | The **sanguine** horror consumed vast amounts of blood.
mi moku e moku **pi telo sijelo** li pilin pona. | I savored the **sanguine** delicacy.

eng: (of media) the act of making fun of the subject, usually to highlight problems with keeping or changing it
examples: lawa ma mi li ike suli ni la, kulupu Anjen kin li ken ala **toki ike** wawa e ona. | My government is so evil, not even The Onion can make good **satire** of it.
ona li **toki** e ni: ma li supa! | He **said** that the Earth was flat!

ni li jan pi **kulupu sona** mi. | This is my schoolmate.
tan jaki Kowi la **kulupu sona** ale li lon ilo li lon tomo ala. | Due to Covid, all schools are virtual and not in physical buildings.

jan lili mi li tawa **tomo sona** kepeken ilo pi sike tu. | My child goes to **school** using a bicycle.
tenpo pini la mi **kama sona lon tomo** lon poka pi jan lili ante. | In the past, I learned at **school** with other children.

tok: sona (pi ijo lon, pi ale lon)
eng: the natural sciences; the objective study of nature

examples: sina pali e pan la, sina ken pona tan lipu sona **tan ala pilin**. | Baking is an exact **science**.
mi tawa lon selo **telo** kepeken supa. | I'm travelling the **seas** on a raft.
o pana e waso tawa poki, o **namako** e ona. ni o awen lon poki lete lon tenpo pimeja. | Place the chicken in a container, then **season** it. This stays in the fridge over night.

eng: time of the year defined by similar temperature and/or natural phenomena
examples: **tenpo ni pi tenpo sike** la, kon li lete mute; ko lete li ken kama tan sewi. | In this **season**, the air is very cold, and there can be snowfall.
jan pi soweli tomo o kepeken **supa monsi** ni. | People with dogs should use this **seat**.

eng: after the first but before the third
examples: kulupu poka ona li wile e mani. ni la ona li open e pali ona **nanpa tu**. | To sustain the family she had to take a **second** job.

eng: a very short period of time
examples: o awen lon **tenpo lili** o pilin e sona sin. | Let's pause for a **second** to review what we've learned today.

eng: a piece of knowledge that is hidden
examples: mi ken ala toki e ni. ni li **sona len**. | I can't tell you. It's a **secret**.
mi toki e nimi **len** tawa ona. | I told him the **secret** password.
lipu ni li lipu nanpa wan pi **sewi ala** lon ma Elopa. tenpo pini la lipu ale li sewi. | This was the first **secular** book in Europe. Before that, all books were sacred.

o **awen** wawa e tomo lon ko. | Go **secure** the tent on the beach.
tomo li **awen** pona! | The tent is **secure**!

mi **awen** e mani ale mi lon poki pi kiwen wawa. | I **secure** all my money in a safe.
mani mi li **awen** pona lon poki. | My money is **secure** in the safe.

eng: to ensure, especially in obtaining something
examples: mi o **kama jo** e lipu tawa waso! | I must **secure** airplane tickets!
sitelen ni la sina ken **lukin** e soweli. | In this picture you can **see** a cat.

sina **sona** ala **sona**? | Do you **see** what I mean?
tenpo kama la sina kama **sona**. | One day you'll **see**.

eng: small pebble that plants grow from
examples: **kiwen** ni li ken **mama** e **kasi** sin. | This **seed** can **grow into** a new **plant**.
ona li lukin e sitelen ni: sike soweli li lon lawa jan. ni li **open** e **nasin** lon lawa ona. | She saw photos of a person wearing a collar and it planted a **seed** in her brain.

**lukin la** ona li kama pilin monsuta li kama pilin ike. | He **seemed** both angered and frightened.
ona li kama pilin monsuta li kama pilin ike **tawa lukin**. | He **seemed** both angered and frightened.
sin la ona li kama musi mute sama **ona** pi tenpo pini. | He's back to being his old cheerful **self**.

mi toki tawa mi. | I'm talking to my**self**.
pilin ona la ale ona li ike. | He hates him**self**.

eng: to provide in exchange for monetary compensation
examples: tenpo suno pali la mi tawa tomo ni li **pana esun** e kili mute. | Every day I work, I come to this market stall and **sell** fruits and vegetables.
jan poka li **toki e wawa** pi lipu ni tawa mi. | A friend **sold** me on this book.

eng: to emit one object outwards or to something or someone
examples: mi **pana** e sitelen tawa tawa sina. | I **sent** you the video.
jan li ken ala lukin la **nasin pilin** pi lukin ala li suli. | For someone that can't see, the non-visual **senses** are important.
**sona** ala li lon toki sina anu seme? | What you're saying doesn't make any **sense**!
mi **pilin** e ike taso lon tomo ni a! | I **sense** nothing but evil in that house!
**toki** ni li pakala ala. | There are no errors in this **sentence**.
sina ike la mi pani e kili jaki tawa sina. | I **sentence** you to rotten tomatoes for your crimes.

eng: earnest or sincere as opposed to being humorous or deceiving
examples: ona li musi ala. ona li toki e lon tawa sina! | He is being **serious**. He's telling you the truth!
ijo mute pi **suli** wawa la mi ken toki musi taso. | There are things that are so **serious** that you can only joke about them.

eng: to perform some purpose or role
examples:
sinpin ni li awen e ma la ma li tawa ala tomo mi. | This wall **serves** to hold the ground up, so it doesn't slide toward my house.
ona li **lon** kulupu utala lon tenpo sike tu tu. | They **served** in the military for four years.

eng: strike a ball to initiate a round of a sport
examples: ona li **pana** wawa e sike la jan ante li lukin ala e ona! | He **served** the ball so hard, the other guy didn't even see it!
o **pana** e moku ni tawa supa lon poka pi open tomo. | **Serve** this food to the table near the front.
pilin mi la mi wawa lon musi ni. taso ona li **anpa** wawa e mi. | I thought I was good at this game, but I got **served**.

eng: [waiter]; person who serves food at a restaurant
examples: tomo ni la, **jan pana** li pona tawa jan ale. | The **servers** are nice to everyone here.

eng: remote computer hosting an online site or application
examples: **ilo weka** pi kulupu Amason li pakala sin! | The Amazon **servers** are down again!

eng: local or physically present hosting computer
examples: mi kepeken **ilo** sina tan ni: ona li wawa a li ken **lawa** e musi. | We'll use your computer as the game **server** since it's beefy enough.
supa moku mi li tan **kulupu sama**! | My dinnerware is from the same **set**!

eng: math concept, collection of unique items
examples: **kulupu** la ijo ale o ante. | In a **set**, all objects must be different.

o **awen** e poki sina lon supa noka. | **Set** your bag on the floor.
mi **pana** e lipu ale tawa supa pali. | I **set** all my documents on my workbench.
jan lawa li **ante** e wawa seli lon tomo. | The boss **set** the temperature in the office.
**unpa** li pona tawa jan mute. | Many people enjoy **having sex**.
waso la **nasin sijelo** li ken ante ale lon tenpo. | Chickens' **sex** can change completely over time.
sama **kala suli** la mi alasa! | Like a **shark,** I hunt!
o sona: kasi ni li **jo e nena** mute ni: ona li ken open e selo sina! | Beware, this plant is very **sharp,** and can pierce your skin!

eng: an edge able to be used to cut or slash
raised in pitch by exactly a semitone

examples: **ona** li wile ala kama sona. | **She** doesn't want to study.
**meli** li uta suwi e mije. | **She** kissed him.

eng: receptacle or apparel for the foot
examples: o esun: **poki noka** tawa lili. sin ale. | For sale: baby **shoes.** Never worn.

eng: small in size, particularly in height
examples: jan poka mi li **lili**. | My brother is **short**.
tenpo ale la sina moku e telo suwi. **o** moku e telo pi suwi ala. | You drink juice all the time. You **should** drink water.

eng: as far as I know; expectation from previous knowledge
examples: **sona mi la** tenpo ni la ona li kama. | He **should** be there by now.
tenpo ale ala la soweli li **pana** e olin tawa jan ona. taso ona li awen olin. | Cats don't always **show** their love and affection, but they care.
sina nasa. jan ale li **sona** e ni. | You're out of touch, and it **shows**.

eng: entertaining performance, seen in person or broadcast
examples:
tenpo lon la **sitelen** musi li lon ala lon ilo pana? | Are there any good **shows** on TV these days?
jan mute li kama li lukin e **musi**. | A lots of people attended the **show**.

eng: to clean one's body with running water, usually while standing or sitting. (cf [bathe])
examples:
jan Pasi li **telo e selo** ona. | Patty **showered**.
mi o **kama kala**. taso ni li pini la mi kama sin. | I have to **take a shower** real quick, but after that's done I'll come back.

eng: a receptacle for cleaning one's body (cf [bath])
examples: mi lon **poki telo**. | I'm in the **shower**.

eng: a burst of something falling like a shower of rain
examples: kiwen pimeja li tawa wawa lon kiwen walo la, seli **lili mute** li kama. | When the flint strikes the steel, it creates a **shower** of sparks.
jan esun li **pana** e mani **mute** tawa ona. | The investors **showered** them with money.

eng: a party held to celebrate an important event in someone's life (e.g. getting married, having a child)
someone who shares at least one parent, usually two
examples: **jan sama** mi li sona e toki Epanja. | My sibling knows Spanish.

eng: someone who shares at least one parent, usually two, but is specified to be nonbinary
examples: mi olin e **tonsi sama** mi. | I love my **nonbinary sibling**.

eng: a portion of a surface or volume
examples: mi o pana e pan tawa **poka** seme supa? | What **side** of the tray should I put the cookie on?

eng: an edge of a two-dimensional shape
examples: sitelen ni la **linja** tu tu li lon. | This figure has four **sides**.
o sona e **ken** pona! | Look on the bright **side**!

eng: part of a dish not the main entree
examples: ona li pana e pan. **poka** la kasi en kili li lon. | She served rice with vegetables and fruits on the **side**.
sina **pana e nimi** sina tawa lipu ni la sina kama jo e tomo ni! | If you **sign** this document, the home will be yours!|

eng: Hand shape and motion representing an idea in non-verbal language
examples: mi tawa ni e **luka** mi la ni li toki e nimi 'kala'. | This sign communicates the word 'fish'.
noka soweli li lon ma ni la mi **sona** e ni: soweli sina li lon poka. | The paw prints on the ground here are a **sign** that your dog is nearby.

eng: Whether a number is positive or negative

examples: **sitelen** ni li toki e nimi 'suno'. | This **sign** indicates the word 'sun'.

eng: An upright written document, generally on a hard material such as wood or metal.
examples: **lipu sinpin** li pana e sona ni: tenpo pimeja la o weka! soweli alasa li ken lon. | The **sign** says that you should leave at night, because there could be wolves.

tok: wile e wawa lili, wile e tenpo lili, wile e pali lili
examples: musi ni li **wile e tenpo lili** a. | This puzzle is quite **simple**.
ilo ni li **lili**, taso ona li pana e sona mute. | This program is **simple**, but it outputs a lot of information.
len li jo ala e sitelen e ijo **nasa** la, ona li pona tawa mi. | I prefer **simple** clothes.
mi lon kulupu **mu.** kulupu ni la jan li nasin e kulupu kepeken luka. | I'm in a **singing** group. In this group, the conductor conducts us with her hand.

eng: someone who shares at least one parent, usually two, but is specified to be female
examples: jan Pita en **meli sama** ona li kama li jo e len mani. | Peter and his **sister** arrived clad in expensive attire.

eng: to be in a state of rest with the buttocks against a horizontal surface
examples: mi **monsi** e supa. | I'm **sitting** on a table.
jan ale li weka wawa tan **ma** pi pakala suli. | Everyone fled from the **site** of the explosion.
poki la, pan suwi **luka wan** li lon. | There are **six** donuts in the box.

eng: the amount of space something takes up
to assesss the efficacy or power of something or someone
examples: weka tomo la jan pi pali ike li **alasa e wawa** pi jan noka. | Outside, criminals **size up** pedestrians.

eng: the outer layer of an animal (including humans), fruit, or vegetable
examples:
mi wile weka e **selo** pi kili ni la mi wile e wawa mute. | If I wanted to take the **skin** off this fruit, I'd need to be very strong.
mi anpa tan ilo tawa la **selo** mi li kama pakala. | I fell off my bike, so I got a cut in my **skin**.

eng: to rest in a state of reduced consciousness
examples: mi **lape** ala la lawa mi li kama nasa. | **Sleep** deprivation makes me go loopy.
pimeja pini la mi tu li **unpa** lon tenpo nanpa wan. | Last night we **slept** together for the first time.

eng: (of an electronic device) to enter a low-power state
happening over a long period of time
examples: jan li wile kama sona e toki sin la ni li **wile e tenpo suli**. | Learning a new language is a **slow** process.

eng: only able to move with little speed
examples: akesi pi **wawa ala** li kama lon pini nasin la soweli wawa li awen lape lon insa. | As the **slow** tortoise finished the race, the quick hare was still sleeping in the middle.
suno mute la ijo **musi ala** li lon. | Many days were **slow**.
soweli mi li moku e telo walo **lili** tan poki. | My pet aardvark drinks a **small** portion of milk from a saucer.
mi **pilin e kon** pan la sijelo mi li kama wile a moku. | When I smelled the rice, I got really hungry.
**kon** jaki ni li seme | What is that gross **smell**?

eng: a facial expression of happiness, in which the sides of the mouth are turned up
examples: mi toki e nimi ona la ona li pana e **sinpin pi pilin pona**. | When I said her name, she lit up with a **smile**.

eng: atmospheric water that froze into ice crystals forming small white flakes
examples: **ko lete** li kama la jan ale li pilin pona. | Everyone is happy when **snow** comes.
sewi li **pana e ko** suwi. | It's **snowing** prettily.

tok: ni la, tawa ni, tan ni
examples: mi wile kama sona e ijo waso. **ni la** mi tawa tomo lipu. | I wanted to learn about ornithology, **so** I went to the library.
lon **a**. | You're **so** right.
My mom can cook, and **so** can my dad. | mama meli mi li ken pali e moku. mama mije mi **kin** li ken.

tok: ko, ko pona, ko pi weka jaki
eng: a thick substance used for cleaning
examples:
sina telo e luka sina la o kepeken **ko**. | When you wash your hands, use **soap**. 
**ko** laso li ken **weka e jaki** tan ilo moku. **ko** walo li ken **weka e jaki** tan luka. | The blue **soap** is for washing dishes. The white *soap** is for washing hands.
o esun e **ko pona** sin lon tenpo poka. | Purchase more **soap** soon.

tok: pi wile toki, pi wile kulupu
examples: tenpo lon la mi **wile** ala **toki** tawa jan mute. | I'm not feeling too **social** right now.
jan lawa sin li wile kama ante e **nasin kulupu**. | The new mayor is pushing for **social** change.
**ilo toki** li kama lon tomo ale la jan mute li kama **kulupu** lon ona. | As computers entered every household, many people started to gather on **social media**.
jan Soka li toki e ni: "mi lon **kulupu.**" | The Joker says that we live in a **society.**

eng: not firm; changing its shape when touched
examples: mi wile lape lon **ko** lon ala kiwen. | I want to sleep on something **soft**, not something hard.
suno **suwi** li lon ale tomo. | There was a **soft** glow filling out the room.
mije ale pi kulupu mi li kama **wawa ala**! | The men of this generation have gotten so **soft**!

eng: someone who fights a war on the ground
examples: **jan utala** mute li moli tan utala pi wile ala. | Many **soldiers** die in unwanted wars.
sina wile ala wile e telo? | Would you like **some** water?
jan li lanpan ala lanpan e ni? | Did **someone** take this?
mi wile e **ijo** ni: ona li pona tawa soweli mi. | I want **something** that'll please my pet.

eng: at multiple periods or instances with low to moderate frequency
examples: **tenpo** la mi moku e kala. taso ona li pona pi suli ala tawa mi. | **Sometimes** I eat fish, but it's not amazing.

tok: jan ni: mi mama e ona
examples: **mi mama e** jan Kiso. | Quiztoc is my **son**.
mi alasa **kalama** kepeken **uta** mi la mi pana e kalama ike. | When I try to **sing**, it comes out wrong.
ale o kama pona lon **tenpo kama poka**. | May everything get better **soon**.
**tenpo lili kama** la, jan olin sina li lon poka sina! | **Soon** your partner will be with you!
mi weka tan sina li tawa ma Tawi lon **tenpo poka**. |  I will leave you and go to Thailand **soon**.

eng: brief acknowledgement of one's mistake or misbehavior

tok: o toki sin (e ni), mi kute ala (e ni)
pilin ike tan pakala, sona e pakala
eng: remorseful, understanding one's own mistakes or misbehaviour
examples: sina utala e mi la, sina kama **sona** wawa **e pakala** sina. | If you try to cross me, you'll be real **sorry**.

eng: empathetic, understanding of someone else's suffering

examples: o **nasin** e lipu kepeken nimi ona. | Please **sort** the books by name.

eng: to some extent but not entirely; partially, somewhat
examples: mi ken sona **lili** e toki ni. | I **sort of** understand that comment.

eng: similar to another kind of thing

examples: **lili la** kili loje li ken **sama** kiwen | Apples can be **sort of** like rocks.
sina jan pi **nasin** seme / sina jan **seme**  |  What **sort of** person are you?

eng: the core essence of something alive
examples: sijelo li moli la **kon** li weka. | When the body dies, the **soul** leaves it.
**jan** pi mute seme li lon ilo tawa? | How many **souls** are on board?
nasin ni li **ken ala pakala**. | This method is sound.
ona li awen **pona** lon pini utala. | She remained safe and **sound** after the conflict.

eng: an auditory sensation caused by vibrations in the air
examples: ona li kute e **kalama** la ona li alasa e tan. | When he heard the **sound**, he searched for the source.
kalama ona li suwi a **tawa kute**. | His music **sounds** beautiful.

eng: liquid with vegetables used as food
examples: mi pana e **kili** ante mute tawa insa **telo** li seli e ona li moku e ona. | I prepared a **vegetable soup** and ate it.

eng: taste associated with vinegar and citrus fruits
equatorial (in relation to the Northern hemisphere)
examples: ma Sonko la **ma seli** en ma lete la pan li ante. | The **south** of China produces a different kind of grain than the north.

eng: polar (in relation to the Southern hemisphere)
examples: sina lon ma Oseja li tawa **lete** la sina kama lukin e waso nasa. | If you travel **south** from Australia, you'll encounter penguins.

tok: ma mun, ma pi kon ala, ma pimeja
examples: mun pi mute a li lon weka **pimeja**. | There are many stars in **outer space**.
pakala la **weka sitelen** tu li lon insa pi nimi 'jan' pi nimi 'suli'. o pona e ona. | There are, mistakenly, two **spaces** in between 'old' and 'person.' Fix it.

eng: to communicate, especially using one's voice
examples: sina wile e ijo mi la o **toki** a! | If you want something from me, **speak**!
nimi 'mi' en nimi 'sina' li **nasa**: ona la nimi 'li' o weka. | The words 'mi' and 'sina' are **special**: with them, the word 'li' gets dropped.

sina **suli** a tawa mi. | You're really **special** to me.
pilin ona la len sin ona li **suli** e ona. | That guy thinks he's **special** because of his new jeans.
mi wile tawa tomo moku **ni a** pi ma Palata. | I want to go to this **specific** Indian restaurant.
mi sona ala. o **toki mute** e ona. | I don't understand, be more **specific**.

eng: (of a word) write or say the letters of
examples: jan li **sitelen** seme e nimi 'kijetesantakalu'? | How do you **spell** 'kijetesantakalu'?

mi poki e jan pona kepeken **wawa** mi. | I trapped the heroes in a cage using my **spell**.
mi toki e **nimi wawa** li kama e soweli mi. | I uttered the magic **spell** and summoned my horse.
mi pana e **wawa lete** tawa anpa la kiwen lete suli li kama. | I cast an **ice spell** to the ground and a big chunk of ice appeared.
mi esun e len sin **kepeken mani** mute. | I **spent** a lot of money on new clothes.
tenpo pi pali ala la mi tawa ma Panse. | We **spent** our holiday in France.

eng: large, tabular document on a computer
examples: mi pana nasin e sona ale tawa **lipu suli**. | I gathered all the information in an organized fashion in a **spreadsheet**.

jan lili li lape lon poka pi **nasin telo lili.** mi kama lukin e ni lon sinpin sina: ona li moku mute e telo. | The child slept next to the **spring.** I saw all over their face that that they had drank a lot of the water.

tok: tenpo pi kama kasi, tenpo pi kama seli, tenpo kasi
eng: the time of year when warmth causes plants to sprout from the earth
examples: tenpo lete li kama pini la **tenpo kasi** li kama a! | When the winter ends, the **spring** begins!

eng: a component used to hold and release energy, used in all sorts of contraptions
examples: ilo sitelen mi li jo e **sike** lon insa. mi luka e sewi ilo la **sike** li esun e ni: ilo li ken ala ken sitelen? | My pen has a **spring** in it. When I press the top of the pen, the **spring** swaps out the pen's ability to write.

eng: in the shape of a square

examples: jan li tawa wawa musi e sijelo tan kalama la jan pi pana kalama li kama pilin e ni: **supa** pi anpa ona li tawa. | The audience was going so wild over the music that the performer could feel the **stage** rattle beneath them.

eng: to be in an upright position while not moving
examples:
jan luka li **awen** lon poka supa. | Five people were **standing** around the table.
jan li **lon** poka tomo li pana e lipu. | There was someone **standing** by the bank and handing out leaflets.

tok: {ijo} li ike ala, utala ala tan ...
examples: kalama pi ma ni li **ike tawa** mi. | I can't **stand** the music in this place!
sina pana e lipu kalama tawa **supa**. | You're putting the note sheet onto the **stand**.
mi esun e moku lon **tomo** moku. | We bought some hot dogs from the hot dog **stand**.
o **sewi e sijelo** sina. | Would you please **stand up**.
**mun** li suno wawa lon tenpo ni. | **The stars** are bright tonight.

eng: concave pointy shape, usually with five points
examples: **sitelen mun** li lon selo lipu. | **A star** was on the outside of the book.

eng: a celebrity, usually an actor or musician
examples: **jan wawa** li mu wawa tawa kulupu suli a! | The **star** sang to the gigantic crowd!
lon **tenpo open** pi pali mi ni la mi sona e ala a. | At the **start** of my career, I knew jack squat.

mi o **kama** pali e moku! | Let's get **started** with cooking!
sina wile ala wile **kama** kalama lon kulupu tawa musi? | Do you want to **start** a band?

eng: to turn on a machine or program
examples: mi **open** e musi Manka la ale la ona li pini tan pakala. | Every time I **start** Minecraft, it ends up crashing.

jan li ken ala lon tomo **pi tenpo lon**. | You can't really enter the building **in its current state**.
mi ken ala ken pona e kalama pi ike ni...? | I don't know if I can save my song from this awful **state**...
jan ilo li lukin e sina la ona li weka e **nasin** lape li kama lon **nasin** utala. | When the AI robot sees a player, it changes its **state** from idle to attacking.

eng: a government or the place that it rules
examples: sewi o wawa ala! **lawa** o wawa ala! mije o wawa ala! | No gods, no **state**, no patriarchy!

utala pi lupa ma o **awen** lon lupa ma. | What happens in the pit **stays** in the pit.
mi wile a awen, taso sijelo li wile lape. | I really want to **stay**, but I've gotta catch some z's.

eng: to cook over boiling water in the air
examples: o pana ala e kili tawa telo. o seli e telo, o pana e kili tawa sewi telo. | You don't boil the vegetables. You boil the water, then **steam** the vegetables over it.
ma ni pi kasi suli li seli mute li telo mute la **kon kin li telo mute**. | The rainforest is so hot and wet that even the air is thick with **steam**.
ona li anpa lon musi lon tenpo mute la ona li **pilin utala** a. | He got beat so many times in his game, he was **steaming**.

eng: one of many tasks needed to reach a goal
examples: mi kute e ale pi nasin lipu. taso mi sona ala **pali** nanpa pini. | I've followed the instructions all the way, but I'm struggling with the **last** step.
nasin ni la **supa** wan li pakala. | One of the **steps** is broken in the staircase here.
sina **awen** a pali anu seme? | Are you **still** working?
mi sona ala e toki ni, taso mi **awen** sona e toki ante. | I don't know this language, but I **still** know other languages.
anpa nena la sike li kama **tawa ala**. | At the bottom of the hill, the ball became **still**.
mi **moku** ike la **insa** mi li ike. | I ate something bad and my **stomach** is upset.

tok: weka e pilin, kama pilin ala
examples: jan li toki e moli ona tawa mi la mi pilin ike suli. mi ken ala **weka e pilin** ni. | I couldn't **stomach** the news of him dying.
sinpin **kiwen** li pini e nasin. | A **stone** wall blocks the way.

mije anu meli ale pi toki moli anu wawa nasa o moli. ona o kama moli tan **pana kiwen**. telo sijelo ona li lon ona. | A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be **stoned** to death; their blood is upon them.
ilo li pana e suno loje la o **kama tawa ala**. | If the traffic light is red, **stop**.
telo sewi li **pini**. | The rain **stopped**.
**tomo** ni **awen** li wile e supa sewi. | This bus **stop** needs a roof.

eng: to not continue to do something
examples: pipi li lon pan. o **awen ala** moku e ona! | There are bugs in the cake. **Stop** eating it!
tan seme la sina esun e len lon **esun** moku a!? | Why are you buying clothes at the soup **store**!?

tok: (pana tawa) poki, poki e; jo, lon
examples:
mi o **pana** e kili ni tawa **poki** o moku lon tenpo kama. | Let's **store** these veggies for later.
poki ni li **jo** e len mi. | This drawer is where I **store** my clothes.
mi o pana e **toki**. | Let me tell you a **story**.
luka ona li **sama palisa.** | Her arms were **straight.**

eng: attracted to the gender opposite one's own (cf '[gay]')
examples: mi **mije** li **olin e meli**. | I am a **straight** man.

eng: long and flexible object, often used to make clothes

examples: jan **wawa** o jo e supa ni o tawa e ona. | We need a **strong** person to pick up this cupboard and carry it.
mi pilin e suwi **wawa** lon kon. | The air had a **strong** sweet scent.

eng: a mass of one or more objects or materials
examples: **ijo** mute li lon tomo mi la o kama ala. | There's a lot of **stuff** in my apartment, so don't come over.

eng: to give in to the will of another
examples: mi **anpa** tawa wile pi akesi suli. | I **submit** to the will of the great dragon.

eng: to give or turn in something in response to a request or demand
examples:
awen la sina **pana** ala e pali sina tawa mi! | You still haven't **submitted** your homework to me!
o **pana** e nimi ken akesi lon ni. | Please **submit** your tortoise name ideas here.

ilo pakala sina en ilo **sama** li ken ala lon tomo ni. | Your slingshot and other **such** devices are not allowed at school.
**ni** li nasin pipi. | **Such** is the way of the worm.
a, pan ni li pona **wawa a**! | Mmh, this is **such** a good cake!
**nasin ken** li ni: mi meli e e mije ni lon wile ona ala. | I **suggest** that we forcefem this man.

eng: the hottest season of the year
examples: mi weka tan tomo lon **tenpo seli** la mi telo e len ale mi! | When I leave my home in the summer, I sweat through all my clothes!

eng: the star closest to Earth; sunshine, sunlight
examples: **suno** li kama pini la mute suno li kama lili. | When the **sun** starts setting, the daylight will run out.
palisa suli mute li **awen** e nasin ni lon sewi. | Many pillars **support** this bridge.
sina wile ni la, mi **pilin sama** li toki e ni tawa jan lawa. | If you want to do this, I'll **support** your decision and tell the officials.
sina wile tawa tomo sona ni la mi wile **pona** e wile sina. | If you want to go to this college, I want to **support** your wishes.

eng: the outer layer of an object
examples: tan seme la **selo** pi sike ni li nasa tawa pilin? | Why does the **surface** of this ball feel weird?

eng: the top layer of the Earth
examples: tenpo mute la pipi linja li awen lon anpa ma, taso tenpo la jan li ken lukin e ona lon **selo**. | Most of the time, worms stay underground, but occasionally, you can see them on the surface.

eng: the top side of a flat object
examples: mi pana e telo ni tawa poki la, **selo** li pana e sike lili mute. | When I pour this drink into a cup, the **surface** emits lots of little bubbles.

eng: to rise to the surface (of a pool or body of water)
examples: ilo anpa li **tawa sewi telo**. | The submarine **surfaced**.
sona sin li **kama lon** li ike lon e jan pi ken ike. | New evidence **surfaced** that pinned the blame on the suspect.

eng: emotion felt when something unexpected happens
examples: jan tu pi kulupu mi li kama mama e jan la mi **pilin nasa a tan sona sin ni**. | I was really **surprised** when my friends told me they were having a baby together.

tok: ijo ni: mi sona ala e kama ona
examples: toki pi mama mi la **ona li pana ala** e ijo tawa mi lon suno mi. suno mi li kama la ona li **pana a** e kiwen luka suwi. | My parents gave me a **surprise** bracelet for my birthday.
sina wile tawa lon supa telo la, sina o sona **tawa lon insa telo**. | If you want to go surfing, you should know how to **swim**.
jan poka mi li wile awen sona e ijo la ona li kepeken **nasin** pona tawa ni. | My friend has a good **system** for when he wants to remember something.
ona li kepeken **ilo** Linu lon tenpo ale. | They daily-drive a Linux **system**.

eng: a plural group in one brain
furniture with a top side for various uses, especially food
examples:
o pana e **moku** tawa **supa**. | Please set the dishes on the **table**.
mi tu li awen lon poka **supa**. | The two of us sat at the **table**.

tok: ken ala; (ijo) li ike (tawa kulupu), (ijo) o len
examples: toki unpa li **ken ala** lon kulupu mute. | Sex is a **taboo** topic in many communities.

eng: a cord-like appendage on something's rear
examples: soweli mi li pilin pona la **linja monsi** ona li tawa wawa. | When my dog is excited, it wags its **tail**.
mi **kama jo** e pan tan mama. | I'll **take** some of my father's bread.

eng: to acquire sth., and remove it from somewhere (take away)
examples: mi **weka e** ken sina pi pali pan. | I've **taken** away your baking privileges.
mi **sin** e pilin sina lon mi. | I'm gonna **take** that opinion as my own.

**pilin** mi la jan ale o weka tan tomo lon tenpo suno. | My **take** is that everyone should leave the house when the sun is out. 
mi toki tawa jan San. **toki** nasa ona li utala e mi. | I talked to John and his hot **takes** disturbed me.
suno en mun li **toki** lon sewi. | The Sun and the Moon were **talking** in the skies.
esun pi mute ala li pana e len tawa jan **pi sijelo suli**. | Few stores sell clothes for **tall** people.
o **moku lili** e pan suwi ni a! | **Taste** this cookie!

kili loje li lon **uta** mi la mi **pilin** jaki a. | The **taste** of strawberries is disgusting to me.
pan li pona ala pona **tawa uta** sina? | Do you like the **taste** of bread?

eng: set of things liked by someone
examples: kalama jaki taso li **pona tawa** ona. | She has a horrible music **taste**.
mi kama **wile** moku e telo kili pi pona mute. | I have developed a **taste** for fine wine.
mi musi **open** taso li pilin e ni: ona li pona ala tawa mi. | I got a **taste** of the game and felt that it wasn't for me.
nasa la pan ni li sama kili **tawa uta**. | Strangely, this bread **tastes** like a fruit.
tenpo ala la mi **pilin** e ijo sama lon **uta** mi. | I've never **tasted** something like this before.
mi mama e **kasi** lon ma suli. | We own a **tea** plantation.

eng: the dried and crushed leaves of this plant
examples: mi wile ken pali e telo seli la mi o esun e **ko** sin. | I've gotta buy some new **tea** if I wanna keep making it.

eng: hot liquid made with the leaves

jan li alasa unpa ike e sina la o toki tawa **kulupu** awen. | If someone tries to touch you inappropriately, talk to the security **team**.

examples: jan Pola li lukin e **lipu** ona li pilin ike. | Flora looked down at her **test** and felt terrible.

eng: to try something to see if it works
examples: mi **alasa** kepeken ilo nasa sina. | I'll **try out** your weird invention.

ale la sina suwi tawa jan ale pi kulupu sina! **ni li pona mute tawa mi**. | **Thank you so much** for always being so kind to everyone around you!

sina pali e moku **pona**. | **Thanks** for cooking.
wawa!! tomo mi li kama pona sin! ni li ken **tan** pali sina taso. | Amazing!! My house is clean again! And it's all **thanks to** your help.

**ni** li pona a! | **That**'s good!

ona li toki e **ni**: telo mute li kama. | They said **that** it will rain a lot.

o lukin! mi lon tomo sama len tomo. | Check it out, I'm in **the** house like carpet.

lipu sina li **lon** supa **ni**. | Your book is on the table **there**.

ale la nasin mute **li lon**. | **There are** many approaches to everything.
soweli mi li **lon** ala la mi pilin ike. | I get sad when my dog isn't **there**.

kili **poka ni** li pona tawa mi. ona weka li ike. | I like **these** fruits better than the ones over there.
pilin **ni** mi li seme? | What are **these** emotions I'm feeling?

waso ni li tawa ala lon sewi. **ona** li tawa lon telo. | Penguins don't fly. **They** swim.

examples: **kulupu** li wile suli e tomo ona la ona li pali e tomo sin poka. | **They**'re building an annex to extend the house

mi lukin e **ijo** nasa lon tomo. ona li seme? | I saw this weird **thing** on the street. Do you know what it is?

eng: a unit e.g. a container of something

eng: to be of an opinion, to believe

a, sitelen **ni** li suwi a tawa lukin. | Wow, **this** poster looks really nice.

**ilo** Kuko | (the **website** called) Google
